issue_title: test_loss.test_squared_hinge_loss has fixed seed that can mask flakiness
issue_body:
The unit test in title have been using fixed seed to mask flakiness. Suggested action:
1. Evaluate whether the test is flaky without fixed seed. If not, remove seed. Else move to 2
2. If test is flaky, determine whether it's an actual uncaught edge case. If so, fix the operator. Else move to 3
3. If numerical instability is inevitable, adjust tolerance level appropriately.

**mxnet** uses **ps-lite** as its parameter server in distributed environment. Currently **ps-lite** only supports integer keys. There are people asking support for string key, but got no response yet.

**mxnet** has its own way to solve this. I have noticed that there are two dicts to do the convert between string key and int key in `kvstore_local.h`. But every worker starts with index zero when converting string to int. `KVStoreDist` inherts from `KVStoreLocal`, it uses the same way to handle string keys.

So is there any possibility that different worker will assign differnt string key to the same int and cause  an error to parameters?

If we do data parallelism, every worker has the same logic, it seems ok. But **mxnet** supports dynamic graph computation, each training sample may has different computation graphs. So the string key pushed to server may have different sequence.

issue_title: What is the difference between nd.concatenate and nd.concat?
issue_body:
I am using Python mxnet 0.12 version

I built a model and used the nd.concatenat when training

The following error appears:
mxnet.base.MXNetError: [12:09:34] src/imperative/imperative.cc:249: Check failed: AGInfo::IsNone(*(outputs[i])) Inplace operations (+=, -=, x[:]=, etc) are not supported when recording with autograd.

Then I changed nd.concatenate to nd.concat, and the error does not appear.

So what is the difference between the two functions? Thanks

issue_title: Conversion from Gluon block to MXNet module failed
issue_body:
Could you please give me some advice about how to fix my python script for converting a neural network model from Gluon block to MXNet module? ## Description of the problem My script can successfully downloaded a ResNet-18 model from Gluon model zoo as a Gluon block object and converts it to a MXNet module object. But when the module object uses its bind method to create memory for the downloaded neural network model, a Runtime error is issued. ## Environment info. * MXNet version 1.0.0 * Python 3.6.3 * Ubuntu 14.04 ## Build info. * Compiler ( gcc 4.8.5 ) * (MXNet 1.0.0)[https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/tree/1.0.0] * Build command `make -j8 USE_OPENCV=1 USE_CUDA=1 USE_CUDA_PATH=/usr/local/cuda-8.0 USE_CUDNN=1` ## My python script I modified [dmlc/nnvm/tutorials/from_mxnet.py](https://github.com/dmlc/nnvm/blob/0aa95413c3394452d2abb30a100639e193516552/tutorials/from_mxnet.py) to confirm if the same thing can be done without NNVM. `from_gluon_model_zoo_to_mxnet_module.py` ```python:from_gluon_model_zoo_to_mxnet_module.py # some standard imports import mxnet as mx import numpy as np ###################################################################### # Download Resnet18 model from Gluon Model Zoo ###################################################################### from mxnet.gluon.model_zoo.vision import get_model from mxnet.gluon.utils import download from PIL import Image from matplotlib import pyplot as plt block = get_model('resnet18_v1', pretrained=True) img_name = 'cat.jpg' synset_url = ''.join(['https://gist.githubusercontent.com/zhreshold/', '4d0b62f3d01426887599d4f7ede23ee5/raw/', '596b27d23537e5a1b5751d2b0481ef172f58b539/', 'imagenet1000_clsid_to_human.txt']) synset_name = 'synset.txt' download('https://github.com/dmlc/mxnet.js/blob/master/data/cat.png?raw=true', img_name) download(synset_url, synset_name) with open(synset_name) as f: synset = eval(f.read()) image = Image.open(img_name).resize((224, 224)) def transform_image(image): image = np.array(image) - np.array([123., 117., 104.]) image /= np.array([58.395, 57.12, 57.375]) image = image.transpose((2, 0, 1)) image = image[np.newaxis, :] return image x = transform_image(image) print('x', x.shape) ###################################################################### # Convert Gluon Block to MXNet Symbol ###################################################################### def block2symbol(block): data = mx.sym.Variable('data') sym = block(data) args = {} auxs = {} for k, v in block.collect_params().items(): args[k] = mx.nd.array(v.data().asnumpy()) return sym, args, auxs mx_sym, args, auxs = block2symbol(block) ###################################################################### # Prepare MXNet module ###################################################################### mx_sym = mx.sym.SoftmaxOutput( data=mx_sym, name='softmax') model = mx.mod.Module( symbol = mx_sym, context = mx.gpu(), label_names = ['softmax_label']) eval_data = x eval_label = np.array([0.0]) batch_size = 1 eval_iter = mx.io.NDArrayIter( eval_data, eval_label, batch_size, shuffle=False) model.bind( data_shapes = eval_iter.provide_data, label_shapes = eval_iter.provide_label ) model.set_params( args, auxs) # --> Runtime Error !! ###################################################################### # Time measurement for MXNet ###################################################################### print("Time measurement starts for mxnet itself..") start_time = time.time() mx_output = model.predict( eval_iter).asnumpy() elapsed_time = time.time() - start_time print("elapsed time:{0}".format( elapsed_time) + "[sec]") top1 = np.argmax(mx_output.asnumpy()) print('MXNet prediction top-1:', top1, synset[top1]) ``` ## How my scripts issued a Runtime error I ran the following command, 
$ python mxnet100/example/gluon/from_gluon_model_zoo_to_mxnet_module.py
And got this error. 
/home/m-ito/anaconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/urllib3/contrib/pyopenssl.py:46: DeprecationWarning: OpenSSL.rand is deprecated - you should use os.urandom instead
  import OpenSSL.SSL
x (1, 3, 224, 224)

issue_title: MXNet: Using FusedRNNCell with its "bidirectional" flag turned True, can lead to hanging of training run.
issue_body:
## Description
MXNet
Using FusedRNNCell with its "bidirectional" flag turned True, can lead to hanging (i.e. infinite pause without progress/error/crash) of training run.

## Details
I am running a single training run of a Sequence-to-Sequence model using the BucketingModule. Iam using an Encoder-Decoder network. I am using a FusedRNNCell with its "bidirectional" flag turned on for the Encoder and an unfused RNNCell for the Decoder.
GPU utilization is 15000MB / 16000MB. CPU utilization is 95%.
For each batch during training, I do a forward() pass and a backward() pass. After a 5-15 epochs, the training run gets stuck in the forward() pass of one of the mini-batches. The forward pass does not complete. No errors are thrown nor does anything crash. GPU/CPU utilization remains identically the same.

I have tried an ablation of many-many things in my training run (architecture, data, code etc). The conclusion is that specifically using the FusedRNNCell with the "bidirectional" flag turned True causes this problem.

Python

issue_title: graph_models doesn't handle unicode verbose_names correctly
issue_body:
When using the `-n` flag in `graph_models` to use the `verbose_name`s of the models and fields, it doesn't handle unicode strings properly when they contain _actual_ non-ascii characters.

## How to reproduce the error

    mkdir test_extensions
    cd test_extensions/
    mkvirtualenv test_extensions
    pip install django django_extensions pygraphviz
    django-admin startproject test_extensions .
    ./manage.py startapp test_app

Now add the following to `test_app/models.py`:

```python
class MyTestModel(models.Model):

    year = models.PositiveIntegerField(
        verbose_name= u'A├▒o'
    )

    class Meta:
        verbose_name = u"MyTestModel with a├▒o"
        verbose_name_plural = u"MyTestModels with a├▒o"
```

Add `django_extensions` to `INSTALLED_APPS` and then try to run:

    ./manage.py graph_models -g -o myapp_models.png -R -S -n -a

This is the error that appears:

```

issue_title: Put the file name in the warning message so I don't need to look at the stack trace
issue_body:
The name of the file where the code triggering the warning is should be part of the warning message, either at the beginning or at the end so it can easily be spotted.

Quoting dmethvin from another ticket:
> To keep the message readable and short I think it should just be the file name part, not the full URL. The docs in warnings.md should also be updated to indicate the file name is in the message.

Use case: sometimes you have warnings (elt's consider the case of warning about deprecated stuff that still works) due to third party libraries that you're never going to fix, so they make a lot of noise that you can't get rid of, while you are trying to spot the migration issues in your own code.

It's painful to have to inspect the traces one by one.

issue_title: Prices allow to use scientific notation of 0
issue_body:
There is one corner case, when using decimals.

```python
>>> Decimal('0') / Decimal('0.1')
Decimal('0E+1')
```
Anyway it's zero, but it has different notation.
```python
>>> result = Decimal('0') / Decimal('0.1')
>>> result == Decimal('0')
True
```

It may cause some problems, when we do some divisions and create `Price` from division results.

```python
>>> result = Decimal('0') / Decimal('0.1')
>>> price = Price(result, currency='EUR')
>>> price.net, price.gross
(Decimal('0E+1'), Decimal('0E+1'))
```

issue_title: python-http-client should define it's own error class which can be imported by consuming libraries
issue_body:
#### Issue Summary

If the response is not success (i.e not HTTP2xx) the library throws a urllib HTTPError.
For clients consuming this library, they now need to know that it was implemented in urllib, import that module into their application and handle the error.

Ideally, a consuming application only needs to know about python-http-client and not how urllib was implemented in order to handle common place http errors.

#### Steps to Reproduce

1. make an api call that returns anything other than HTTP2xx
2. attempt to handle error raised without importing urllib and without doing a catch all.


#### Technical details:

* python-http-client Version: master (latest commit: [commit number])
* Python Version: all

issue_title: Opening row fails if List name contains non-URL safe characters
issue_body:
If the name of the List contains a non-URL safe character, SharePointListRow.open() raises an HTTPError due to a Bad Request response.

The culprit is that the List's title is not passed through urllib.quote() when building the URL.

The current implementation of `external_system_identifier` has some short comings:
* [ ] currently considers only the first `035__a` and `035__9` losing any additional value.
* [ ] in case value is not set, it still adds an entry with the schema (producing invalid record).
* [ ] exporting back to MARC associates to $a and $z only based on a whitelist of schemas.

So e.g. in record https://inspirehep.net/record/700376 there:
```
<datafield tag="035" ind1=" " ind2=" ">
<subfield code="9">OSTI</subfield>
<subfield code="a">892532</subfield>
</datafield>
<datafield tag="035" ind1=" " ind2=" ">
<subfield code="9">OSTI</subfield>
<subfield code="z">897192</subfield>
</datafield>
```

The second OSTI is currently lost.
Builder isn't populating `license.material`, `license.imposing` fields.

As discussed in upstream bug report saltstack/salt#38230 pygit2 tries to authenticate indefinite with wrong credentials instead of failing after two consecutive failures with same authentication token as specified by https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-3.1
Possible a libgit2 Bug if it's reproducable with other language bindings.


## Testcase:
### nginx dummy server configuration
This is just a stub configuration, no real repository needed
````
location ~ /git(/.*) {
        add_header "WWW-Authenticate" "Basic realm=\"Closed Areas\"" always;
        return 401;
    }
````
### test client
````python
#!/bin/python2.7

import pygit2

username = 'dummyUser'
password = 'dummyPassword'
credentials = pygit2.UserPass(username, password)
callbacks = pygit2.RemoteCallbacks(credentials)
pygit2.clone_repository("http://git.demo.local/git/test.git", "test.git", bare=True, callbacks=callbacks)

````

browser is a ``splinter.Browser()`` instance with the firefox driver. ```python >>> browser.find_link_by_partial_text("admin").text u'admin DEV' >>> browser.find_link_by_partial_text("admin DEV") [] ``` The html to reproduce this issue ```html admin DEV ```

I am using a recipe as suggested by Mike bayer https://bitbucket.org/zzzeek/sqlalchemy/wiki/UsageRecipes/SessionModifiedSQL. To  create and update sharded tables in the same database. His recipe works absolutely fine with sessions, when the same is added to a scoped session managed by pyramid_tm transactin manager, then it just rolls back the transaction at the end of request for no reason.

```

with session_shardid(DBSession,table_hash):	
			Base.metadata.create_all(DBSession.connection())
                        self.request.tm.commit()

*model.py*
DBSession = scoped_session(sessionmaker(extension=ZopeTransactionExtension()))

@listens_for(DBSession, "after_begin")
def _connection_for_session(session, trans, connection):
    """Share the 'info' dictionary of Session with Connection
    objects.
    This occurs as new Connection objects are associated with the
    Session.   The .info dictionary on Connection is local to the
    DBAPI connection.
    """
    connection.info['session_info'] = session.info

@contextmanager
def session_shardid(session, shardid):
    """Apply the "shard" id to all SQL emitted by the given Session.
    """
    session.info["shard"] = shardid
    yield
    del session.info["shard"]

@listens_for(Engine, "before_cursor_execute", retval=True)
def _apply_shard_id(connection, cursor, statement, parameters,
                                        context, executemany):
    """Apply a "shard id" to statements.

    Similar to the comment listener, we alter the statement on the
    fly replacing occurrences of "_shard_" with the current "shard id".

    """
    session_info = connection.info.get('session_info', {})
    if "shard" in session_info:
        statement = statement.replace("_shardid_", session_info["shard"])
        for param,value in parameters.items():
        	if isinstance(value, str):
        		parameters[param] = value.replace("_shardid_",session_info["shard"])

    return statement, parameters

`ykman piv generate-csr` seems to generate certificate signing requests with wrong signatures
issue_body:
I generated a key on my Yubikey using `ykman` as follows:
```bash
ykman piv generate-key 9a client.key
```

Then, I generated a certificate signing request in order to obtain a valid certificate from a CA:
```bash
ykman piv generate-csr 9a client.key client.csr -s "my-subject"
```

When I try to sign the certificate signing request, I obtain the following error from OpenSSL:
`Signature did not match the certificate request`

I use the following command for OpenSSL:
```bash
openssl x509 -req -days 365 -sha256 -in client.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -set_serial 1 -out client.crt
```

The tool `yubico-piv-tool` seems to work just fine:
```
yubico-piv-tool -a generate -s 9a -o client.key
yubico-piv-tool -a verify -a request -S "/CN=my-subject" -s 9a -o client.csr -i client.key
```
Now OpenSSL is able to correctly sign the certificate request.

issue_title: Concordance: have separate tokens for search and for display?
issue_body:
<!--
This is an issue template. Please fill in the relevant details in the
sections below.
-->

##### Text version
<!-- From menu _OptionsŌåÆAdd-onsŌåÆOrange3-Text_ or code `orangecontrib.text.version.full_version` -->
0.2.5

##### Orange version
<!-- From menu _HelpŌåÆAboutŌåÆVersion_ or code `Orange.version.full_version` -->
3.7.dev

##### Expected behavior
Can browse by hashtags or emojis in Concordance.


##### Actual behavior
Since Concordance has internal preprocessing to display proper sentences, it cannot find Twitter-unique tokens, such as hashtags and emojis.


##### Steps to reproduce the behavior
Twitter - Preprocess Text (tweet tokenizer) - Word Cloud + Concordance. When selecting a hashtag in WC, it is not displayed in Conc.


##### Additional info (worksheets, data, screenshots, ...)
We need to discuss how to solve this.
- If we allow user-defined tokenization in Concordance, then it won't display logical sentences.
- If we override any tokenization with our internal one, the user can't find hashtags or emojis (as one would expect from tweet tokenizer).

Option: have an additional input for Preprocessor, which would override default tokenization.
Problem: Preprocess does not output Preprocessor.

Option 2: provide a checkbox in Concordance that would disable default preprocessing and take tokens from the input.
Problem: Concordance can now again display junk, but at least that's what the user consciously wanted...? ­¤śü 

Option 3: implement clever matching, partial matching? Also would we be able to display full text without resorting to tokens? How or what do you match then and how do you construct the view?

issue_title: If --boilerplate option is used boilerplate name from helper settings is overiden with default
issue_body:
if you use --boilerplate option get_boilerplate_settings will return the default value for ``ALDRYN_BOILERPLATE_NAME`` which would be 'bootstrap3', and settings are updated with default value even if helper settings contains other boilerplate name.

Hotfix for me was to add a check if default_settings at that point already has a boilerplate name - remove the default value (as it is done with other default values) :

    # Do not override helper settings with defaults.
    if 'ALDRYN_BOILERPLATE_NAME' in default_settings.keys():
        del boilerplate_settings['ALDRYN_BOILERPLATE_NAME']

The code was inserted here https://github.com/nephila/djangocms-helper/blob/develop/djangocms_helper/utils.py#L266

Will do a PR with this fix.

Writing trove classifiers is painful for two reasons:

- you need to know and use the :: delimited values
- some fields are already defined in other options passed to setup()

I'd like to propose a new way to write classifiers in setuptools, based on a mapping that
will generate the list for you -- given a simplified list of classifiers, 
and that will set a few extra metadata fields automatically.

Taking back the example from pypa sample setup.py:

```python
    classifiers=[
        # How mature is this project? Common values are
        #   3 - Alpha
        #   4 - Beta
        #   5 - Production/Stable
        'Development Status :: 3 - Alpha',

        # Indicate who your project is intended for
        'Intended Audience :: Developers',
        'Topic :: Software Development :: Build Tools',

        # Pick your license as you wish (should match "license" above)
        'License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License',

        # Specify the Python versions you support here. In particular, ensure
        # that you indicate whether you support Python 2, Python 3 or both.
        'Programming Language :: Python :: 2',
        'Programming Language :: Python :: 2.7',
        'Programming Language :: Python :: 3',
        'Programming Language :: Python :: 3.3',
        'Programming Language :: Python :: 3.4',
        'Programming Language :: Python :: 3.5',
]
```

would become 

```python
    classifiers={'status': 'alpha', 'audience': developers', 'topic': 'build tools', 
                         'license': 'MIT', 'python_versions': ['2.7', '3.3', '3.4', '3.5']}
```

This new version will generate the previous one **and** set the license  & the **requires-python** options.  Each trove classifier will have a corresponding short name - as far as I can see it's easy to build a unique list of short names for classifiers, that are easier to use in setup.py files

issue_title: Add a doc page for application composition
Allow custom escaping functions when generating LDAP filters
issue_body:
Certain LDAP implementations have different escaping requirements for various attributes. For example, in Active Directory the `objectGUID` attribute must have its value in hexadecimal representation. Here is a concise description of the issue: https://support.pingidentity.com/LDAP-Filter-on-ObjectGUID

In `ldaptor` it currently looks something like this:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python

from ldaptor.protocols import pureldap


filt = pureldap.LDAPFilter_equalityMatch(
    attributeDesc=pureldap.LDAPAttributeDescription('objectGUID'),
    assertionValue=pureldap.LDAPAttributeValue('90395F191AB51B4A9E9686C66CB18D11')
)

print filt.asText()
```

And we get the following result:

```
$ python escape.py 
(objectGUID=90395F191AB51B4A9E9686C66CB18D11)
```

Since this value is automatically escaped there's no opportunity to perform the hexadecimal escaping. If we try that beforehand the value will be double escaped. My question is, do you know of any good way to perform some kind of custom escaping of an attribute value? If not, I'd like to purpose a change that would allow for this.

I purpose all `LDAPFilter_*` classes take an optional argument when initialized that allows for custom escaping. It would look something like this:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python

from ldaptor.protocols import pureldap


filt = pureldap.LDAPFilter_equalityMatch(
    attributeDesc=pureldap.LDAPAttributeDescription('objectGUID'),
    assertionValue=pureldap.LDAPAttributeValue('90395F191AB51B4A9E9686C66CB18D11'),
    escaper=OBJECTGUID_ESCAPE_FUNCTION
)

print filt.asText()
```

Which would produce:

```
$ python escape.py 
(objectGUID=\90\39\5F\19\1A\B5\1B\4A\9E\96\86\C6\6C\B1\8D\11)
```

But, `escaper` could be any escaping function. In this scenario we've just defined some `OBJECTGUID_ESCAPE_FUNCTION` function that does what we need for `objectGUID`. Finally, if no `escaper` function is specified we simply fallback on the original behavior.

I was originally planning on simply submitting a PR with the intended changes, but I made the issue first in case you were aware of some way to do this without any changes to the code base. If that's not the case I'll get to work on submitting a PR :)

Let me know if you have any concerns!

issue_title: What is the best way to manage the inputs from more than one bolt?
issue_body:
Is there a standard method to manage the inputs from more than one bolt?

I have three bolts (A, B, C) that do something. Then there is bolt MERGE that should merge A, B and C emits and output merge result.

[I'm using a RAM storage in MERGE bolt](https://github.com/SpamScope/spamscope/blob/v1.0/src/bolts/json_maker.py#L111-L125), and I waiting tuple from A, B, C. But is this the best way?

In streamparse 3.2 it doesn't look like there is first class support for cycles as there was with the clojure dsl.

If you are to attempt to define a cycle in the naive way, you will reference a variable before it is defined because declaration of inputs happens at class instantiation time. _this works for all graphs that can be topologically sorted_:
```python
	word_spout = WordSpout.spec()
	count_bolt = WordCountBolt.spec(inputs={word_spout: Grouping.fields('word')}, par=1)

	a_bolt = A.spec(name="A", inputs=[count_bolt["count->A"], b_bolt["B->A"]]) # here this fails because b_bolt is not defined
	b_bolt = B.spec(name="B", inputs=[c_bolt["C->B"]])
	c_bolt = C.spec(name="C", inputs=[a_bolt["A->C"]])
```

The current solution therefore is to init each bolt and then extend inputs:
```python
def extend_inputs(bolt, inputs):
	additions = bolt._sanitize_inputs(inputs)
	bolt.inputs = {**additions, **bolt.inputs} # only python >3.5
	bolt.common = ComponentCommon(inputs=bolt.inputs, streams=bolt.outputs, parallelism_hint=bolt.par, json_conf=bolt.config)

class WordCount(Topology):
	word_spout = WordSpout.spec()
	count_bolt = WordCountBolt.spec(inputs={word_spout: Grouping.fields('word')}, par=1)
	a_bolt = A.spec(name="A", inputs=[count_bolt["count->A"]])
	b_bolt = B.spec(name="B")
	c_bolt = C.spec(name="C")

	extend_inputs(a_bolt, [b_bolt["B->A"]])
# and so on...
```
Is there a better way to do this currently?

In December 2016 @joleroi @astrofrog and I had a Google hangout where we discussed `astropy.regions`, the minutes are here:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1u-WjsP4wLWx4g1-om7imstjngOmwpAJQ8EyVZxvhxV4/edit#heading=h.wf1hdss3eefb

I dropped the ball on implementing the changes discussed there, but I'd like to come back to that now. Actually, if this is non-controversial, I'd like to make the changes this week, before the regions 0.2 release, because it's not a lot of work, just removing a bunch of stuff instead of leaving dummy methods that return `NotImplementedError` at the moment.

Before going ahead, I'd like to ask @astrofrog and @joleroi again, and also @keflavich and @larrybradley for input!

---

Basically we said that `area` and `contains` should be removed from the base sky region class:
http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/regions.SkyRegion.html

The reason is that `area` and contains` aren't well-defined concepts for all sky regions.
We argued about this for quite a while, but in the end I think there was agreement that it's not well-defined and thus doesn't belong in the base class.
E.g. how exactly would you implement `area` and `contains` for
http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/regions.RectangleSkyRegion.html
so that it's valid for very large rectangles (say width = 100 deg)?

So we'd like to propose basically that we adopt the DS9 model of having regions mainly defined as pixel regions, and having sky regions mostly as convenience, but not with separate definitions of `contains` methods. Instead `contains` would be implemented in the following generic way on the base class for sky regions, and only be well-defined if the user has a WCS:

```
class SkyRegion(object):
    def contains(self, sky_coord, wcs):
         Reg = self.to_pixel(wcs)
         Pix_coord = foo(sky_coord)
         Return reg.contains(pix_coord)
```

In the future, we would add the special cases (circle & polygon) back where contains is well-defined in sky coordinates, either as separate classes or on the existing classes.

This brings us to the other major change we'd like to propose, which is to get rid of the different modes in the pixel to sky transformation:
- http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/regions.SkyRegion.html#regions.SkyRegion.to_pixel
- http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/regions.PixelRegion.html#regions.PixelRegion.to_sky

This was never implemented, it was just an idea, there will be no functionality lost.

So for now, we'd remove the modes from the base class, only leaving the "mode" how DS9 transforms back and forth, and we'd remove all the checks and NotImplementedError from the derived classes.

---

Note that `regions` will remain experimental, and based on user and dev feedback, we can always do it differently in the end. So basically -- if there's agreement on this I would make the change tomorrow, before the 0.2 release. And if it's controversial, I would just make the release as-is, and we take our time to discuss here.

I think we should make a regions 0.3 release next week.
We can use this issue to discuss.

There is a 0.3 and 0.4 milestone, so anyone feel free to add or move issues / PRs to those:
https://github.com/astropy/regions/milestones

There already are some new features and fixes since the 0.2 release, and more are coming this week. A release is not much work and in my experience helps to focus the work and make sure discussions conclude, issues get closed and PRs finished and merged.

I would prefer if someone else does it! @joleroi @keflavich ?
If no, I could do it Thursday, May 18. Let me know.
In any case, I can spend a few hours to work on `regions` early next week.

If some has plans to work a lot on `regions` in the coming weeks, we can also postpone for a few weeks. (But why do 0.3 next week and 0.4 in a month or two?)

issue_title: API: replace visualise by visualize in hog() to respect our recommendations
issue_body:
Needs a deprecation cycle... See the documentation for that.

Fix the spelling in the documentation as well.

While I was building http://feram.co/scikit-image-cheatsheet I noticed several inconsistencies in the scikit-image API. I'll just layout how I would restructure the API. Sorry for any duplicates with other issues and this should probably be split into several issues later ­¤śģ­¤ść.

# Inconsistent argument names

## `image` vs. `img` vs. `im` vs. ŌĆ”

I think it should be image everywhere.

Outliers:
- [ ] `segmentation.find_boundaries`
- [ ] `segmentation.mark_boundaries`
- [ ] `transform.hough_ellipse`
- [ ] `transform.hough_line`
- [ ] `transform.hough_line_peaks`
- [ ] `transform.integral_image`
- [ ] `draw.set_color`
- [ ] `feature.daisy`
- [ ] `feature.draw_multiblock_lbp`
- [ ] `feature.multiblock_lbp`

I probably missed a few ...


## label vs label_image

I'm in favor of `label_image`, but I figured pythonistas like ~unreadable~ concise shortcuts. I guess label is good as well ŌĆ”


## `row` vs. `r`vs.`rows`vs.`rr`

Definitely `row`, `rows`, `col` & `cols` (Actually I'd prefer `column` and `columns`, but I guess that's too long for python =D)

## There are definitely more inconsistent argument names I just can't remember them right now ...

# Create new `shapes` module

There are so many shapes in the morphology module. I think they deserve their own module. I found kind of surprising to found the shape methods intermingled with the morphology operations.

This means we would have `shape.{cube,diamond,disk,octagon,octahedron,rectangle,square,star}`


# Create new `binarize` module

While it makes sense to be able to retrieve the threshold generated by a certain thresholding algorithm, I think most people will simply want to use it to binarize their images. That's why a propose a module corresponding to the thresholds.

- `binarize.isodata(image)`
- `binarize.li(image)`
- `binarize.local(image)`
- `binarize.niblack(image)`
- `binarize.otsu(image)`
- `binarize.sauvola(image)`
- `binarize.triangle(image)`
- `binarize.yen(image)`
- `binarize.all(image)`

They expect an (grayscale) image and return the binarized image. Simple as that.

Also the thresholds should probably be moved to their own module as well.
I mean you already prefix all of them with `threshold_`. Why not use `threshold.` and make it a proper module? ­¤śä
Btw: The API of the thresholds is also kind of chaotic. Some return images, some simple values. There should be at least an argument flag for each one to always return an image.

issue_title: BUG: skimage fails on missing docstrings under python -OO
issue_body:
When trying to bytecompile skimage with the -OO option using cx_freeze/py2exe 
the _parse_docs function  on line 605 in [skimage/measure/_regionprops.py](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/master/skimage/measure/_regionprops.py) crashes because 

    regionprops.__doc__ is None.

The reason is that Python code byte-compiled with -OO has doc-strings stripped out (i.e. docstrings are None).

This creates problems for skimage when the function _parse_docs tries to extract the properties from the documentations of the regionprops function.


## See also:
http://bugs.python.org/issue23189#
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/22299532/unsupported-operand-types-for-nonetype-and-str-winappdbg-error-after-c

I made this naive hack by creating a `.copy()` of the labels variable in the function. Now each core can modify the `labels` value.

```
...................
# In the case of labels, recursively build and return an output
    # operating on each label separately
    if labels is not None:
        labels_copy=labels.copy()
        label_values = np.unique(labels)
        # Reorder label values to have consecutive integers (no gaps)
        if np.any(np.diff(label_values) != 1):
            mask = labels >= 1
            labels_copy[mask] = 1 + rank_order(labels[mask])[0].astype(labels.dtype)
        labels_copy = labels.astype(np.int32)

        # New values for new ordering
        label_values = np.unique(labels_copy)
        for label in label_values[label_values != 0]:
            maskim = (labels_copy == label)

..................
```

Sorry if it is not elegant (labels can be a big matrix) but I though to bring up the issue because I think that it will be really useful to be able to run this function in parallel.

Thanks

issue_title: Rotate Accepts in range [0,128) and returns in range [0, 1.0]
issue_body:
## Description
Rotate accepts input with RGB bytes and silently transforms it into floats in the range [0, 1].
I think either:
  Rotate and other transform methods that accept byte range values should warn when doing so, because the output format is changed.
Or
  Rotate and other transform methods that accept byte range values should return in the same format as the input.

If you guys can confirm which of these behaviors is preferred, or why the silent change is a feature and not a bug then I can either submit a pull request or go away.

from skimage.transform import rotate
import numpy as np

img = np.random.uniform(size=(10, 10)) * 128
img = img.astype('byte')
print(np.mean(img))
>> 68.8

img = rotate(img, 30)

>> 0.47

issue_title: Improve documentation on predictedSegmentDecrement parameter in TM
issue_body:
Incorporate Subutai's email in the documentation for TM:

>With random input there's a chance that any given column A can follow another column B.  The probability of this is roughly tied to the sparsity (i.e. 2% usually). The purpose of this parameter is to remove segments that get added and reinforced over time because of these chance occurrences.
>
>My feeling is that it should just a bit larger than (the column-level sparsity * increment). So, if our column-sparsity is 2% and our permanence increment is 0.01, this parameter should be something like 4% * 0.01 = 0.0004
>
>--Subutai

issue_title: Last_modified field lost in response when PATCH has no change
issue_body:
Bug found while porting *Reading List* to *Cliquet* 2.

Since ``id`` and ``last_modified`` are not in schema anymore, they are dropped during ``resource.apply_changes()``, and if storage is not used (i.e. when no field is changed), then they are not restored, and thus not served.

```diff
@@ -701,7 +710,7 @@ class BaseResource(object):
         updated.update(**changes)
 
         try:
-            return self.mapping.deserialize(updated)
+            deserialized = self.mapping.deserialize(updated)
         except colander.Invalid as e:
             # Transform the errors we got from colander into Cornice errors.
             # We could not rely on Service schema because the record should be
@@ -709,6 +718,11 @@ class BaseResource(object):
             for field, error in e.asdict().items():
                 raise_invalid(self.request, name=field, description=error)
 
+        deserialized[self.collection.id_field] = record[self.collection.id_field]
+        deserialized[self.collection.modified_field] = record[self.collection.modified_field]
+
+        return deserialized

```

issue_title: add_view; includeme not working as expected
issue_body:
Following a minimal example of a app structured with `includeme` and `add_view`. I marked the parts with `FIXME` that are not working like expected (thats how pyramid also allows them).

*demoapp/__init__.py*:
````python
from pyramid.config import Configurator

def main(global_config, **settings):
    config = Configurator(settings=settings)
    config.include("cornice")
    config.include("demoapp.views.includeme")
    # FIXME shouldn't be necessary -> config.scan("demoapp.views")
    return config.make_wsgi_app()
````

*demoapp/views.py*:
```python
from collections import defaultdict
from pyramid.view import view_config
from cornice import Service

_USERS = defaultdict(dict)

# FIXME this should also work in includeme
user_info = Service(name='users', path='/{username}/info')

def get_info(request):
    username = request.matchdict['username']
    return _USERS[username]

def set_info(request):
    username = request.matchdict['username']
    _USERS[username] = request.json_body
    return {'success': True}

def who(request):
    return {"usernames": _USERS.keys()}

def includeme(config):
    # FIXME user_info should be defined here
    user_info.add_view('post', set_info)
    user_info.add_view('get', get_info)

    config.add_route("who", "/who")
    config.add_view(who, route_name="who", renderer="json")
````

The easiest solution would be, to add `config` as a paramater to Service!?

issue_title: Documentation no longer specifies HAE vs MSL for POS_LLH (0x0201)
issue_body:
In v1.0 of SBP.pdf, bit 3 of the flags in POS_LLH (0x0201) differentiated between WGS-84 (the height above a mathematically defined perfect ellipsoid that GPS uses internally) and height about the mean sea level (the height above a wobbly, bumby, ball of mass that smoothly follows the geoid).  I've seen the difference between the two systems vary between 5 to 30 meters.

In v2.0, this was removed from the documentation.  Bit 3 is labeled "Reserved".

It seems pretty important to convey which mode is being output.

issue_title: dnanexus java api: infinite socket timeouts
issue_body:
DXSearch call can hang in the ``SocketInputStream.socketRead0()`` if network connection is unstable.
The root cause is that DXHTTPRequest creates Apache HttpClient with default parameters. As result the infinite socket timeout is used.

The issue can be reproduce with a synthetic test case:
```
    @Test(expected = DXHTTPException.class)
    public void test() throws Exception {
        new Thread(() -> {
            try {
                System.err.println("start server thread");
                new ServerSocket(17291).accept();
                System.err.println("accepted");
            } catch (Exception e) {
                throw new RuntimeException(e);
            }
        }).start();

        DXEnvironment environment = DXEnvironment.Builder.fromDefaults()
                                                         .setApiserverHost("localhost")
                                                         .setApiserverPort(17291)
                                                         .build();

        DXSearch.findDataObjectsWithEnvironment(environment).withClassRecord().execute().asList();
    }
```
Expected result: The ``DXHTTPException`` exception should be thrown.
Actual result: Test hangs with stacktrace:
```

	at java.net.SocketInputStream.read(SocketInputStream.java:170)

	at org.apache.http.impl.conn.DefaultHttpClientConnectionOperator.connect(DefaultHttpClientConnectionOperator.java:141)

I ran into a couple of cases where `svg2rlg.Renderer.render` fails with

> object of type 'map' has no len()

Converting map objects to lists in about 5 places resolved the problem, e.g.:

```
points = map(parseLength, re.split('[ ,]+', points))
```
becomes
```
points = list(map(parseLength, re.split('[ ,]+', points)))
```


I can put together a pull request if you like.

issue_title: `unregisterAdapter` uses '==' while the underlying adapter registry uses 'is'
issue_body:
In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.interface/+bug/551049, @mgedmin reported:

>Try this:

    class MyTestClass(PlacelessSetUp):

        def _handler(self, event):
            print "Got event!"

        def test(self):
            sm = zope.component.getGlobalSiteManager()
            sm.registerHandler(self._handler, required=(zope.interface.Interface, ))
            assert sm.unregisterHandler(self._handler, required=(zope.interface.Interface, ))
            zope.event.notify(object())

> The assertion will demonstrate that the global site manager thinks it successfully unregistered the adapter, yet the subsequent notify() will invoke our handler.

> Why? Because self._handler creates a new bound method instance every time you write that expression. sm.unregisterHandler compares it to the previously registered factory object using '==' which returns true. But then the site manager invokes zope.interface.adapter.BaseAdapterRegistry.unsubscribe, which uses 'is' for identity checking and therefore thinks it got a different handler.

> zope.component should use the same kind of check as the underlying implementation, because it now lies to the programmer about unregistering an adapter that really remains registered.

@d03cc followed up:

> Trying to reproduce it, I noticed that the code that does not do the proper unregistration is in zope.interface and not in zope.component

It defines `__nonzero__` to always be True for Python 2, but it doesn't define `__bool__` for Python 3. So it falls back to `__len__` and you get different behaviour (requests seem false when they shouldn't be).

Found testing zc.resourcelibrary with Python 3. https://github.com/zopefoundation/zc.resourcelibrary/pull/2

Both Products.CMFEditions and Products.CMFUid define a Generic Setup default profile with a catalog step (catalog.xml) that creates the cmf_uid index.

However, every time one of these products gets reinstalled, the index is recreated and its contents are lost.

See http://maurits.vanrees.org/weblog/archive/2009/12/catalog for guidelines to avoid this problem.

Travis CI knows about this project: https://travis-ci.org/zopefoundation/Products.CMFCore/branches
But it does not make any builds for it.

Functions addPictureFrom* in odf.opendocument use time.time() to gemerate unique filenames. If the functions are called in a loop, this can result in duplicate names due to limited resolution of time.time() in some systems (tested on old Windows Vista PC).

A possible workaraound is the use of uuid module replacing

manifestfn = u"Pictures/%0.0f%s" % ((time.time()*10000000000), ext)

with

manifestfn = u"Pictures/%s%s" % (uuid.uuid4().hex.upper(), ext)

Currently, pressing Ctrl-C interrupts semaphore_gil by virtue of causing the read to return EINTR. However, there is a race condition where a SIGINT after entering the C++ code but before the read will be missed. It's not too serious because the user can always press Ctrl-C again.

The alternative is to use signal.set_wakeup_fd to have the signal delivered on a file descriptor, and use poll() to wait for the semaphore. That is probably more reliable since it will allow timely delivery even for signals with SA_RESTART specified. Unfortunately, there is only one wakeup_fd for a process, and it's used by trollius/asyncio, so it would be necessary to somehow put a tee in the delivery to the wakeup fd so that both fds get notified; it may also be a problem for blocking waits on the non-default thread.

issue_title: Transition condition can't access User obejct
issue_body:
In order to allow certain transitions only to a set of staff users, I'd like to access the User object inside my customized condition functions.

This unfortunately is not possible, because that function never "sees" the ``request`` object. By patching the templatetag function ``fsm_transition_hints`` and the method ``FSMTransitionMixin.get_transition_hints()`` is would easily by possible to pass to ``request`` object to my condition function.

If you have concerns about backward compatibility with existing condition functions, I could add a boolean named, say ``pass_request`` to the ``custom`` dict. Then the ``request`` object is passed in only when this is ``True``. 

What do you think about this and shall I create a pull request for this feature?

from distutils.core import setup
from Cython.Build import cythonize

)

How to make a apk with a pyx file? Thank you a lot.

When using `.ui` files to build an interface graphically with QtDesigner, it is possible to load the interface directly from the `.ui` file rather than having to transform it onto python then import it. This is already integrated into [PyQt4](http://pyqt.sourceforge.net/Docs/PyQt4) and [PyQt5](http://pyqt.sourceforge.net/Docs/PyQt5/designer.html#PyQt5.uic.loadUi), and relatively easy to implement in PySide as seen in [python_qt_binding](https://github.com/ros-visualization/python_qt_binding/blob/groovy-devel/src/python_qt_binding/binding_helper.py#L196), the concurrent that doesn't support Qt5.

I know it's not the workflow used in spyder, but it would be a great utility for some projects (including mine).

issue_title: Compilation error due to macro BROTLI_ALLOC in enc/memory.h (GCC 7 -Wint-in-bool-context)
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm compiling ngx_brotli, with v0.6.0 brotli release (but the macro is the same in current master branch), and I had to fix an issue with this macro:
```
../ngx_brotli_test/deps/brotli/enc/./block_splitter_inc.h:403:61: error: ŌĆś*ŌĆÖ in boolean context, suggest ŌĆś&&ŌĆÖ instead [-Werror=int-in-bool-context]
     double* insert_cost = BROTLI_ALLOC(m, double, data_size * num_histograms);
../ngx_brotli_test/deps/brotli/enc/././memory.h:43:5: note: in definition of macro ŌĆśBROTLI_ALLOCŌĆÖ
   ((N) ? ((T*)BrotliAllocate((M), (N) * sizeof(T))) : NULL)
     ^
```
Just replace the macro like this:
```c
#define BROTLI_ALLOC(M, T, N)                               \
  ((N) > 0 ? ((T*)BrotliAllocate((M), (N) * sizeof(T))) : NULL)
```
(test if (N) is strictly greater that zero).

I believe this is the fix, it compiles and runs fine after changing this, but only tried using nginx with ngx_brotli (it does not use the decoder part).

I'm using GCC 7.1.1 (as available in ArchLinux repos); nginx does **not** add a parameter like `-std` to commands.

```
cc -c -pipe  -O -W -Wall -Wpointer-arith -Wno-unused-parameter -Werror -g -fPIE -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 -fstack-check -fstack-protector-strong -Wno-deprecated-declarations  -I src/core -I src/event -I src/event/modules -I src/os/unix -I objs -I src/http -I src/http/modules -I ../ngx_brotli_test/deps/brotli/include \
	-o objs/addon/enc/block_splitter.o \
	../ngx_brotli_test/deps/brotli/enc/block_splitter.c
```

ngx_brotli repo actually uses the commit 222564a95d9ab58865a096b8d9f7324ea5f2e03e for brotli, if there is any security concern about this.

If you agree to, I can try to submit a PR (on both repos, in ngx_brotli it's just updating the list of files in enc) so I can have my name somewhere in the history :)

index         'AAPL'         'MSFT'          cash

`SPARQLUpdateStore` is not transaction aware so I wrap it in an `AuditableStore`. The problem is that `AuditableStore`'s commit calls `SPARQLUpdateStore`'s commit, which raises a `TypeError` exception:

```python

# ./fuseki-server --update --mem /auditable_sparql_store

import rdflib
from rdflib.plugins.stores.auditable import AuditableStore
from rdflib.plugins.stores.sparqlstore import SPARQLUpdateStore

store = SPARQLUpdateStore('http://fuseki.til.io:3030/auditable_sparql_store/query',
                          'http://fuseki.til.io:3030/auditable_sparql_store/update')
store = AuditableStore(store)

graph = rdflib.Graph(store, 'http://example.com/')

graph.add((rdflib.URIRef('http://example.com/s'), 
           rdflib.URIRef('http://example.com/p'), 
           rdflib.Literal('')))

graph.commit()
```

raises a `TypeError: The SPARQL Update store is not transaction aware!`

Maybe should we only get a warning instead of an exception?

issue_body:
I was using pneumatk in the past, and now I am trying to transition to NeuroM, but it seems that there is a small difference between the results of pneumatk and NeuroM. Also there is a very big difference between NeuroM h5 file and asc file, I think those files should be identical:

```python

from neurom import ezy
from neurom.features import NEURITEFEATURES, NEURONFEATURES
from neurom.core.types import TreeType


H5pop = [ezy.load_neuron(cell) for cell in ['C050600B1.h5']]
H5neurom = [] 
H5neurom.append([round(sum(NEURITEFEATURES['section_lengths'](i, neurite_type=TreeType.basal_dendrite)),2) for i in H5pop])
H5neurom.append([round(sum(NEURITEFEATURES['section_volumes'](i, neurite_type=TreeType.basal_dendrite)),2) for i in H5pop])
H5neurom.append([round(sum(NEURITEFEATURES['section_areas'](i, neurite_type=TreeType.basal_dendrite)),2) for i in H5pop])

import pneumatk as pn
popPne = [ pn.io.load(cell) for cell in ['C050600B1.h5']]
H5pnue =[]
H5pnue.append([sum(i.get_section_lengths(neurite_type='basal')) for i in popPne])

H5pnue.append([sum(i.get_section_surface_areas(neurite_type='basal')) for i in popPne])

ASCpop = [ezy.load_neuron(cell) for cell in ['C050600B1.asc']]
ASCneurom =[]

import pandas as pnd
pnd.DataFrame([H5pnue, H5neurom, ASCneurom],index = ['h5 pneumatk', 'h5 neurom', 'asc neurom'], columns = ['Basal length' , 'Basal volume', 'Basal area'] )

and 
l2/2012.07.23/morphologies/h5

What do you think?

issue_title: Allow creation of schedules against a job template specifying an inventory
issue_body:
Hi,

I would like to use tower-cli to be able to schedule runs of a job but against different inventories. It seems this is possible using the following command, but unified-job-template requires an integer which won't be easily known:

```
tower-cli schedule create --name="Schedule against inventory" --unified-job-template="5" --inventory-source="My Inventory 1" --rrule="DTSTART:20171215T000000Z RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;INTERVAL=1;COUNT=1"

Resource changed.
```

If I try this against job_template I can enter the job template's name, but the only option I have is specifying an inventory_source which ends up in an error:

```
tower-cli schedule create --name="Schedule against inventory" --job-template="Demo Job Template" --inventory-source="My Inventory 1" --rrule="DTSTART:20171215T000000Z RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;INTERVAL=1;COUNT=1"

Error: More than one unified job template fields provided, please tighten your criteria.
```

Is there another way I should be doing this? Or can a feature please be added to enable creating a schedule using either a name in unified-job-template, or inventory with job-template?

Thanks

issue_title: format id fails when no results are found with schedule list
issue_body:
When searching with schedule list and format human if no results are found that is returned to the user and the exit code is 0
```
$ tower-cli schedule list -Q description 43 
No records found.
$ echo $?
0
```
If you search for an object with format id however an error is generated and the process exits as a failure
```
$ tower-cli schedule list -Q description 43 -f id
Error: Can not use id format when multiple objects are returned.
$ echo $?
62
```

For my use case exit 0 with no output seems correct. The program was able to perform the query against the tower server and it does list all matching ids, there just don't happen to be any. Either way this seems like a bug.

### Description
It is impossible to use `async def` coroutines (https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0492/#new-coroutine-declaration-syntax) for route handler.

`async def` for routes is not working due to connexions decorating of route functions and later testing for `asyncio.iscoroutinefunction()`, which is broken by loosing `object.__code__.co_flags`.
This is not relevant for `@asyncio.coroutine` decorated functions, since they own a `_is_coroutine` attribute, which is copied by `functools.wraps`. See `inspect.iscoroutinefunction()`. 

see:
- `connexion.utils.has_coroutine`
- `connexion.decorators.response.ResponseValidator.__call__`
- `connexion.decorators.decorator.EndOfRequestLifecycleDecorator.__call__`

### Expected behaviour
It works when using `async def`.


### Actual behaviour
 It simple gives a `AttributeError("'generator' object has no attribute 'status'",)` error.


### Steps to reproduce
Use `async def` for coroutines definition.


### Additional info:

Output of the commands:

- `python --version`
Python 3.6.5

- `pip show connexion | grep "^Version\:"`
Version: 1.5.2

issue_title: Endless progress bars for endless (but complete) spark jobs
issue_body:
There have been cases where the Spark StatusTracker believes a job is still in progress when in fact it has completed and returned results to the driver. When this happens (and surely it's some kind of Spark bug that it does), the progress thread continually outputs useless progress information.

It's not clear how to filter out these dead jobs. Minimally we should consider having a way of turning the progress bars off / on at runtime without hacking `sc._jsc = None` to get the thread to quit. This is clearly stopgap: we should figure out what's causing the bug and get it fixed upstream.

issue_title: Passed in configuration values are overridden by the defaults
issue_body:
I wanted to override the default log level sent to Sentry, so I decided to set `logging=False` and pass that into the wrapper. But I noticed that Sentry was still receiving INFO messages and when [digging around the code I noticed this][update]:

```python
self.config.update(config_defaults)
```

It seems like it should be the other way around to work, `config_defaults` should be updated with `self.config`. 

Right now I'm working around this by setting the environment variable `SENTRY_CAPTURE_LOGS` to an empty string since that's falsy in Python, but I see there's another PR that would let me pass in false instead. :)

``` python
def return_jira_keys(
        jira_instance: JIRA,
        jql: str,
        result_set: set,
        start_at: int,
        max_res: int = 500) -> Generator[str, None, None]:
    """
    Returns the JIRA issue ID's within a specified range.
    :param jira_instance: A pre-authed JIRA API object
    :param jql: The JQL string that will be used to search for issue ID's.
    :param result_set: A set that will contain all unique keys, used
    so that multiple ranges of keys can be processed concurrently.
    :param start_at: The starting index for issue ID's
    :param max_res: The maximum number of results to pull in a single request.
    Due to the squirrely nature of JIRA's request handling, it is recommended

    issues = jira_instance.search_issues(
        jql_str=jql,

to use algoliasearch-django in some tests, i must do something like

from django.apps import AppConfig

issue_title: Memory exception during fallback emu should be handled like normal memory exception
issue_body:
If the program crashes during mcore's normal emulator, we detect it, and generate a testcase with an input triggering the crash.

Currently, if the program crashes on an instruction that is handled by the fallback emu (we didn't implement the insn), pending #591 , it would be treated as an `InstructionEmulationError`, which in reality, it should be handled just the case above, and a testcase should be generated.

```
$ manticore fallback_emu_crash power
2017-11-22 15:51:07,147: [123423] m.manticore:INFO: Starting 1 processes.
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "_ctypes/callbacks.c", line 315, in 'calling callback function'
  File "/home/mark/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/unicorn/unicorn.py", line 401, in _hook_mem_access_cb
    cb(self, access, address, size, value, data)
  File "/mnt/hgfs/code/manticore/manticore/utils/emulate.py", line 98, in _hook_xfer_mem
    self._cpu.write_int(address, value, size*8)
  File "/mnt/hgfs/code/manticore/manticore/core/cpu/abstractcpu.py", line 512, in write_int
    self.memory[where:where+size/8] = [Operators.CHR(Operators.EXTRACT(expression, offset, 8)) for offset in xrange(0, size, 8)]
  File "/mnt/hgfs/code/manticore/manticore/core/memory.py", line 852, in __setitem__
    self.write(index.start, value)
  File "/mnt/hgfs/code/manticore/manticore/core/memory.py", line 1039, in write
    super(SMemory, self).write(address+offset, [value[offset]])
  File "/mnt/hgfs/code/manticore/manticore/core/memory.py", line 830, in write
    raise InvalidMemoryAccess(addr, 'w')
manticore.core.memory.InvalidMemoryAccess: Invalid memory access (mode:w) <fffffff8>
```

[fallback_emu_crash.zip](https://github.com/trailofbits/manticore/files/1497440/fallback_emu_crash.zip)

todo
- first, fix #591 
- not entirely sure, but i think this is what needs to happen: here, https://github.com/trailofbits/manticore/blob/master/manticore/core/cpu/abstractcpu.py#L792, catch the exceptions that emu.emulate() can raise related to bad memory accesses () (as opposed to `Exception`). since these exceptions will ultimately stem from `memory.py` (see trace above), it should be fine to let them bubble up, which should trigger the testcase generation code

ItŌĆÖs extremely common to call new_symbolic_buffer/value and then write_bytes/int. Maybe we should add a convenience interface for the client to do this in one call. A consideration for this would be how any manual constraints would be specified for this buffer, since the user never actually has the Expression object to use in a call to the `state.add/constrain` api.

Hello 

what is the current approach to adding an user specific map to a plot?
I'm particular interested in adding topography data (assuming the user has that data) similar to the **plot etopo5 topography** in [basemap examples](http://matplotlib.org/basemap/users/examples.html). That kind of map may the helpful in case of big ground clutters or Anomalous propagation.

https://github.com/opencobra/memote/blob/e3316631a1f8e9d7f0f8ca7f78eb4cc6f184b71c/memote/support/sbo.py#L45

I propose to replace "SBO" with "sbo" in the code above.

My SBO annotations are not recognized because I use lowercase "sbo" as the key, not "SBO". Lowercase [sbo](http://identifiers.org/sbo) is the identifiers.org ([Juty et al. 2012](https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkr1097)) namespace for SBO, and I like to use identifiers.org whenever possible. In particular I like the immediate reference to the ontology used; for example, http://identifiers.org/sbo explains what ontology http://identifiers.org/sbo/SBO:0000249 is from.

I think identifiers.org namespaces are a better alternative than hardcoding "SBO" into sbo.py, and the metabolite and reaction annotation checks all use identifiers.org namespace-identifiers: [pubchem.compound](http://identifiers.org/pubchem.compound) [kegg.compound](http://identifiers.org/kegg.compound) [seed.compound](http://identifiers.org/seed.compound) [inchikey](http://identifiers.org/inchikey) [chebi](http://identifiers.org/chebi) [hmdb](http://identifiers.org/hmdb) [reactome](http://identifiers.org/reactome) [metanetx.chemical](http://identifiers.org/metanetx.chemical) [bigg.metabolite](http://identifiers.org/bigg.metabolite) [biocyc](http://identifiers.org/biocyc) [rhea](http://identifiers.org/rhea) [kegg.reaction](http://identifiers.org/kegg.reaction) [metanetx.reaction](http://identifiers.org/metanetx.reaction) [bigg.reaction](http://identifiers.org/bigg.reaction) [ec-code](http://identifiers.org/ec-code) [brenda](http://identifiers.org/brenda)

@ChristianLieven
Needed for hinting variable fonts where overlaps are kept.

issue_title: bucket.enable_logging should use bucket.name as default prefix
issue_body:
It matches [`gsutil`](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/access-logs#delivery) and is probably what most users want by default.
> By default, the object prefix is the name of the bucket for which the logs are enabled.

Currently it sets an [empty prefix](https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-cloud-python/latest/_modules/google/cloud/storage/bucket.html#Bucket.enable_logging).

If someone stores logs from different buckets in a common *logs-bucket*, without explicitly setting a prefix, they overwrite each other.

issue_title: Speech v1p1beta1 words do not match transcription when diarization enabled
issue_body:
The words do not match the transcription in the LongRunningRecognitionResponse when speaker diarization is enable.  The issue goes away when speaker diarization is disabled.  

I attached a wave file that I used to run the following example
[bug_v1p1beta1.wav.zip](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/files/2291293/bug_v1p1beta1.wav.zip)

```
import os
  
from google.cloud import storage
from google.cloud import speech_v1p1beta1 as speech
from google.cloud.speech_v1p1beta1 import enums
from google.cloud.speech_v1p1beta1 import types

GOOGLE_BUCKET      = os.environ['GOOGLE_BUCKET']

def transcribe(filepath):
    # put audio file into google storage
    storage_client = storage.Client.from_service_account_json('service-account.json')
    google_bucket  = storage_client.get_bucket(GOOGLE_BUCKET)
    blob = storage.Blob(os.path.basename(filepath), google_bucket)
    blob.upload_from_filename(filepath)

    # transcribe file
    gcs_uri = "gs://" + str(blob.bucket.name) + "/" + blob.name
    speech_client  = speech.SpeechClient()
    operation = speech_client.long_running_recognize(
        audio=speech.types.RecognitionAudio(
            uri=gcs_uri
        ),
        config=speech.types.RecognitionConfig(
            encoding=enums.RecognitionConfig.AudioEncoding.LINEAR16,
            profanity_filter=False,
            language_code='en-US',
            enable_word_time_offsets=True,
            audio_channel_count=2,
            enable_speaker_diarization=True,
            enable_separate_recognition_per_channel=True
        )
    )
    operation_result = operation.result()
    blob.delete()

    # print results
    for speech_recognition_result in operation_result.results:
        print('Channel: {0}'.format(speech_recognition_result.channel_tag))
        print('\ttranscription: {0}'.format(speech_recognition_result.alternatives[0].transcript))
        print('\twords: {0}'.format(' '.join([w.word for w in speech_recognition_result.alternatives[0].words])))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    transcribe('./bug_v1p1beta1.wav')
```

This script results in:
```
Channel: 1
	transcription: this is a test of the Left Channel
	words: this is a test of the Left Channel
Channel: 2
	transcription: this is a test of the right Channel
	words: this is a test of the right Channel
Channel: 1
	transcription:  there seems to be a bug in Google Cloud speech v1p one day that one
	words: this is a test of the Left Channel there seems to be a bug in Google Cloud speech v1p one day that one
Channel: 2
	transcription:  this test is meant to recreate that bug
	words: this is a test of the right Channel this test is meant to recreate that bug
Channel: 1
	transcription:  goodbye
	words: this is a test of the Left Channel there seems to be a bug in Google Cloud speech v1p one day that one goodbye
```

The words do not match the transcription and appear to be accumulating by channel or speaker.

When speaker diarization is *disabled*, then the issue does not occur and the following output is produced.
```
Channel: 1
	transcription: this is a test of the Left Channel
	words: this is a test of the Left Channel
Channel: 2
	transcription: this is a test of the right Channel
	words: this is a test of the right Channel
Channel: 1
	transcription:  there seems to be a bug in Google Cloud speech v1p one day that one
	words: there seems to be a bug in Google Cloud speech v1p one day that one
Channel: 2
	transcription:  this test is meant to recreate that bug
	words: this test is meant to recreate that bug
Channel: 1
	transcription:  goodbye
	words: goodbye
```

Running:
```
Mac OSX 10.13.6
Python 2.7.14
google-cloud==0.32.0
google-cloud-speech==0.35.0
```

issue_title: Can Shadowsocks add VPN work mode?

Currently Shadowsocks work in local proxy mode.
But sometimes we need  it serve in overall proxy mode just like VPN, especially in Linux.
eg: When we build docker image, it will quote URL to download but the URL is blocked by GFW, now we need overall VPN mode,local proxy mode is far not enough.
Can Shadowsocks add VPN work mode ?

issue_title: Unable to pass Cluster name as String
issue_body:
When using the ECS `Service` class, I'm unable to pass a simple string name to the `Cluster` property. For example:

    service = Service('Service',
                      Cluster='cluster',
                      DesiredCount=2,
                      LoadBalancers=[ecs_load_balancer],
                      Role='ecsServiceRole',
                      TaskDefinition=Ref(task_definition))

If I do so, I get a type error:

    TypeError: Cluster is <type 'str'>, expected [<type 'basestring'>, <class 'troposphere.Ref'>]

I don't understand why `'Cluster': ([basestring, Ref], False)` is the right type definition for the property ([`ecs.py:23`](https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/blob/78e7a075d461d706661fb5a8156df9a5b9ae7da4/troposphere/ecs.py#L23)). It appears that when passed a `Ref`, the type checking is simply ignored (see [`__init__.py:93`](https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/blob/78e7a075d461d706661fb5a8156df9a5b9ae7da4/troposphere/__init__.py#L93)), so it works in that case. If a string is passed, then it carries on to [`__init__.py:102-105`](https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/blob/78e7a075d461d706661fb5a8156df9a5b9ae7da4/troposphere/__init__.py#L102-L105), where it expects the passed value to be a list. Since itŌĆÖs not, it raises a `TypeError`.

I noticed there are several other places where the `([basestring, Ref], False)` syntax is used both in ECS and elsewhere, and IŌĆÖm new to Python, so IŌĆÖm probably missing something obvious. 

We have separate CloudFormation templates for our clusters and the services running on the clusters, so in the service template IŌĆÖm not sure how I can obtain something `Ref`erenceable to avoid passing the plain string.

[AWS::EMR::Cluster](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-emr-cluster.html)

Use the┬Ā`CustomAmiId`┬Āproperty to specify a custom Amazon Linux AMI for a cluster.

issue_title: There is no way to use less than the default backends without warning.
issue_body:
Right now on master and in 1.2.0rc0 its damned if you do, damned if you don't.

Baseline in Aurora:
```
$ ./pants options --name=default_backend_packages
sys:1: DeprecationWarning: DEPRECATED: option 'default_backend_packages' in global scope will be removed in version 1.3.0.
  All backends must be specified using the backend_packages option. That option has the same defaults as this one, and you can appendand filter those using +[...] and -[...] syntax, as described here: http://www.pantsbuild.org/options.html#list-options.
default_backend_packages = [] (from CONFIG)

$ ./pants options --name=backend_packages
sys:1: DeprecationWarning: DEPRECATED: option 'default_backend_packages' in global scope will be removed in version 1.3.0.
  All backends must be specified using the backend_packages option. That option has the same defaults as this one, and you can appendand filter those using +[...] and -[...] syntax, as described here: http://www.pantsbuild.org/options.html#list-options.
backend_packages = ['pants.backend.codegen', 'pants.backend.graph_info', 'pants.backend.project_info', 'pants.backend.python'] (from CONFIG)
```

Try removing `default_backend_packages: []`:
```diff
diff --git a/pants.ini b/pants.ini
index 6ed4186..c42b43a 100644
--- a/pants.ini
+++ b/pants.ini
@@ -23,3 +23,3 @@ plugins: [
 # TODO(John Sirois): Remove once we upgrade to 1.3.0+.
-default_backend_packages: []
+#default_backend_packages: []
 ```
```
jsirois@gill ~/dev/apache/aurora (master *) $ ./pants options --name=default_backend_packages
WARN] /home/jsirois/.cache/pants/setup/bootstrap-Linux-x86_64/1.2.0rc0/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/bin/local_pants_runner.py:59: DeprecationWarning: DEPRECATED: default_backend_packages option will be removed in version 1.3.0.
  You are relying on the following backends being listed in the deprecated default_backend_packages option: pants.backend.jvm.
  This is probably because you are overwriting the value of the backend_packages option in your pants.ini, instead of appending to it.
  To get rid of this message, consider changing backend_packages: [...] to backend_packages: +[...] in your pants.ini. Once you are happy with the state of your backend_packages option, you can set default_backend_packages to [] to silence this warning.
  options, build_config = OptionsInitializer(options_bootstrapper, exiter=self._exiter).setup()

default_backend_packages = ['pants.backend.graph_info', 'pants.backend.python', 'pants.backend.jvm', 'pants.backend.codegen', 'pants.backend.project_info'] (from HARDCODED)

jsirois@gill ~/dev/apache/aurora (master *) $ ./pants options --name=backend_packages
WARN] /home/jsirois/.cache/pants/setup/bootstrap-Linux-x86_64/1.2.0rc0/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/bin/local_pants_runner.py:59: DeprecationWarning: DEPRECATED: default_backend_packages option will be removed in version 1.3.0.
  You are relying on the following backends being listed in the deprecated default_backend_packages option: pants.backend.jvm.
  This is probably because you are overwriting the value of the backend_packages option in your pants.ini, instead of appending to it.
  To get rid of this message, consider changing backend_packages: [...] to backend_packages: +[...] in your pants.ini. Once you are happy with the state of your backend_packages option, you can set default_backend_packages to [] to silence this warning.
  options, build_config = OptionsInitializer(options_bootstrapper, exiter=self._exiter).setup()

backend_packages = ['pants.backend.codegen', 'pants.backend.graph_info', 'pants.backend.project_info', 'pants.backend.python'] (from CONFIG)
```

Try using `-[...]` list syntax to subtract un-needed backends:
```diff
diff --git a/pants.ini b/pants.ini
index 6ed4186..113b7b8 100644
--- a/pants.ini
+++ b/pants.ini
@@ -23,11 +23,6 @@ plugins: [
 # TODO(John Sirois): Remove once we upgrade to 1.3.0+.
-default_backend_packages: []
-
-backend_packages: [
-    # Pants built-ins:
-    'pants.backend.codegen',
-    'pants.backend.graph_info',
-    'pants.backend.project_info',
-    'pants.backend.python',
-  ]
+#default_backend_packages: []
+
+backend_packages: -['pants.backend.jvm']
+
```
```
$ ./pants options --name=default_backend_packages
WARN] /home/jsirois/.cache/pants/setup/bootstrap-Linux-x86_64/1.2.0rc0/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pants/bin/local_pants_runner.py:59: DeprecationWarning: DEPRECATED: default_backend_packages option will be removed in version 1.3.0.
  You are relying on the following backends being listed in the deprecated default_backend_packages option: pants.backend.jvm.
  This is probably because you are overwriting the value of the backend_packages option in your pants.ini, instead of appending to it.

issue_title: use ParseSearchDirs to find include dirs in the XCodeCLITools and LLVM subsystems
issue_body: In #5998 we started compiling nontrivial C++ sources and realized we didn't have all the include directories we needed on OSX. We were able to get these headers from relative paths in the llvm distribution we provide, but this is potentially fragile. `ParseSearchDirs` was created in #5943 in order to make this sort of searching easier, and I thought I had been using it, but somehow along the way it was removed. We should use the `ParseSearchDirs` subsystem to find the include dirs that we are manually adding to the LLVM subsystem in #5998, and potentially extend that subsystem if necessary (if it can't find everything we need it to).

issue_title: Key name overrides for Python keywords.
issue_body:
Objects cannot have a keyword for a field name. For instance, **_this is invalid_**:

```
@related.immutable
class MyModel(object):
    for = related.StringField()   # invalid !
    not = related.BooleanField()  # invalid too !

```

But sometimes, you need to be able to convert to and from JSON formats that contain those keywords as keys:

```
{ "for": "Elise", "not": true }
```

To handle these situations, this fix will allow for overriding the name in the to/from dictionary methods.

```
@related.immutable
class MyModel(object):
    is_for = related.StringField(key="for")
    is_not = related.BooleanField(key="not")

obj = related.to_model(MyModel, { "for": "Elise", "not": True })
assert obj.is_for == "Elise"
assert obj.is_not is True
```

issue_title: In wily, base layer install hook for uses python3.4, when charms.reactive is installed for python3.5
issue_body:
On wily, 'python3' uses python 3.4 by default.  The install hook from the base layer uses 'python3' in its shebang, which means it runs with python 3.4.  Since charms.reactive is installed for python3.5 (via pip), this fails.

This is true for the other generated hooks too. As far as I can tell, the only way to work around this is to manually edit the generated hooks to use 'python3.5' instead of 'python3'.

removing pair from latter rule

It would be helpful if fontmake/glyphsLib honored the glyphOrder custom parameter in Glyphs. This is useful when working with other tools that have a dependency on glyph order.

E.g. Selawik is hinted in VTT, with hint sources saved out to an XML file so I can re-apply hints anytime I rebuild the font. Trouble is, low-level code (e.g. OFFSET[]) has explicit glyph id references, thus once hinted, I need to preserve glyph order. Any new glyphs need to go at the end of the list so as to preserve the glyph IDs of previously hinted glyphs. So, I need some way to control glyph order.

(And, yes, I'd like to update VTT and it's various high/low level languages to support named references, but that's a really big work item).

issue_title: Run monitor process directly on a remote machine
issue_body:
Request from @jean997 who's trying out cluster executions. Currently the way DSC works is to leave the long running job submission process on a local computer and sends jobs directly to a cluster. The advantage is 1) less burden on the login node and, perhaps more importantly 2) can have the potential to distribute the same benchmark across different, separate HPC systems although we've not implemented that interface yet.

Now there are clear draw-backs -- we'll need a local machine as the "monitor". It is not convenient if people work with a laptop.

So we'll need to take a step back to for something in-between, that is, run the command itself on a remote machine like `snakemake`. Again should not be hard once we decide on a clear syntax of the interface ... I'm going to propose an interface next.

issue_title: Forgetting ==, <= or >= silently overwrites objective function
issue_body:
Whenever one forgets ==, <= or >= in a constraint, the objective function is overwritten without warning. I suggest to issue a warning if an objective function is added to a problem that already has an objective function. Otherwise, when typing a larger model and forgetting the right hand side of a constraint, the result may be very confusing.

Test Case:
import pulp
a = pulp.LpVariable('f^min', lowBound=0, cat=pulp.LpContinuous)
milp = pulp.LpProblem("TC", pulp.LpMinimize)
milp += a
milp += a >= 1
milp += 2 * a -3

Actual Result:
First objective function is silently overwritten with MINIMIZE `2 * a - 3`.

Expected Result:
Pulp overwrites objective function but issues a warning (to avoid confusion).

issue_title: Are there any docs on how Deis handles servers going down?
issue_body:
Hey,

Sorry if this has been asked, or is obvious. I have tried to find some docs on how Deis handles the host machines going down, but I couldn't see anything. 

Say we have 3 host machines, i know that if 1 goes down, it will route the traffic to the other 2, but what happens if 2 go down? Will Deis run on 1 machine for a short time? Or is it like Mongo, and just refuse to do so?

As a developer I would like to set ENV variables across my organization, i.e. NEW_RELIC_LICENSE_KEY so that I don't have to configure it for each application.

Acceptance Criteria:
* New builds will reflect the ENV variable.
* ENV variable can be overridden if set at the application level.

Nice to haves:
Aliasing. It would be great if I could set SOME_VAR=$OTHER_VAR. This would allow me to do NEW_RELIC_APP_NAME=$DEIS_APP.

issue_title: Feature: Allow System & Application monitoring based on Graphite
issue_body:
Warning: I have the feeling there should already be a bug report about this (maybe even commented on by me?) but I couldn't find it (I only looked at open bug reports though...)

As a Deis System Administrator I want to see

* Global (VM) Performance statistics over time (memory, CPU Utilization, Network Usage, (free) disk space). Normally this can be done by any networking system (zabbix), but "auto" configuration should be central (eg adding a node should automatically use this system).
Bonus points if I can add alerts that trigger on certain tresholds..

As a Deis user
* Per container metrics. This includes Memory Usage, CPU Usage, Network Usage etc per container & also averages per container type (web, worker, cmd, ...).
* Per web metrics. On top of that for the web type containers I also want metrics on requests (number of requests, response times, number of failed requests)...
This is similar to what Heroku provides. (https://devcenter.heroku.com/articles/metrics)
* As bonus points I should be able to use the same toolkit for custom metrics (number of logins, ...)

I think graphite together with collectd / statsd and others will be a perfect method to implement this.

Obviously all of this would probably be implemented as optional components that can just run onto the coreos cluster...


Note: I Understand it is not in Deis' scope to write a new monitoring tool that implements storing metrics, triggers etc... I merely propose using existing tools, whereby I think Graphite based stack might be a good start (althoug something else can probably work as well).


If there is any intrest in this I might have a look at it mystelf to build out a basic platform...
