issue_title: Avoid leaking index from training data set as feature, 100% classification accuracy regardless of actual time series
issue_body:
I try to test tsfresh with a simple binary classification using a k-nearest-neighbor-classifier and k-fold-validation:
I always get a classification accuracy of 100%, regardless of the actual time series.
The reason is that in my input data the first 15 time series are labeled as ``type 1`` and the remaining 15 time series are labeled as ``type 2``, with ascending indices.

The problem is that the features selected by `select_features` are `value__index_mass_quantile__q_0.8  value__index_mass_quantile__q_0.7 value__index_mass_quantile__q_0.2  value__index_mass_quantile__q_0.3` and so on, all of them are directly proportional to the `id` in the training data set.

Now the k-nearest-neighbor classifier just has to decide whether these index "features" are above a certain threshold to make a correct classification.

I need to disable the consideration of the index for feature extraction.
Using the index of the samples in my training data as input for feature extraction reduces my model to absurdity.
Therefore all features should be only based on the time stamps and the associated values, but not on the order of the samples in my input data.

How can I disable this incorrect behavior?

``` Python
extracted_features = extract_features(time_series, column_id="id", column_sort="time", column_value="value")
impute(extracted_features)
features_filtered = select_features(extracted_features, y) # use features_filtered and y as input for k-fold validation

issue_title: Add CLI command for entering into a python REPL
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

We need a CLI command for entering into a REPL connected to the chain.

### How can it be fixed

This functionality is likely to evolve but initially, anything that leverages the foundation laid out in #300 to allow the user to use `$ trinity console` which drops them into a python REPL with some objects in the local context which allow them to interact with the chain works for now.

issue_title: Document that $CONDA_BLD_PATH must end with "/conda-bld" (and stop breaking stuff with minor releases)
issue_body:
On September 30, @kalefranz inserted a new assertion (`name == 'conda-bld'`) into `context.py` ([see here](https://github.com/conda/conda/blame/master/conda/base/context.py#L299)) causing `conda info` to fail when `CONDA_BLD_PATH` does not end with `/conda-bld`:
```console
$ CONDA_BLD_PATH=/tmp conda info
An unexpected error has occurred.
Please consider posting the following information to the
conda GitHub issue tracker at:

    https://github.com/conda/conda/issues



`$ /home/user/conda/bin/conda info`




    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/exceptions.py", line 479, in conda_exception_handler
        return_value = func(*args, **kwargs)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/main.py", line 145, in _main
        exit_code = args.func(args, p)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/main_info.py", line 225, in execute
        channels = list(prioritize_channels(channels).keys())
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 380, in prioritize_channels
        channel = Channel(chn)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 161, in __call__
        c = Channel.from_value(value)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 211, in from_value
        return Channel.from_url(value)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 196, in from_url
        return parse_conda_channel_url(url)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 132, in parse_conda_channel_url
        configured_token) = _read_channel_configuration(scheme, host, port, path)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/models/channel.py", line 97, in _read_channel_configuration
        for name, channel in sorted(context.custom_channels.items(), reverse=True,
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/_vendor/auxlib/decorators.py", line 265, in new_fget
        cache[inner_attname] = func(self)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/base/context.py", line 265, in custom_channels
        all_sources = self.default_channels, (self.local_build_root_channel,), custom_channels
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/_vendor/auxlib/decorators.py", line 265, in new_fget
        cache[inner_attname] = func(self)
      File "/home/user/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/base/context.py", line 246, in local_build_root_channel
        assert name == 'conda-bld'
    AssertionError

```
This change was not documented and led to a hard to trace failure of our test suite (we set `$CONDA_BLD_PATH` in our test suite and `conda info` is, e.g., run by `conda build`). 

Unfortunately, this was not the first time that conda/conda-build introduced subtle, but breaking changes in a patch release.

issue_title: Misaligned package sizes in download output
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior During the "Downloading and Extracting Packages" phase of `conda update `, `conda` displays each package, its download size, and its progress bar. When printing the download sizes, It seems to only provide enough space for a four-character long file size, excluding the byte unit (e.g. `12.4 MB`). A five character file size, such as `149.6 MB`, causes the pipe separating the size from the progress bar to misaligned with the preceding and following rows. issue_title: [Multi-user] Permission problems in package caching when mixing with other channels
issue_body:
I am using a multi-user setup when conda is installed into a read-only location and current user cannot write to it. Having this setup, I continue experience issues ever since Conda 4.1 release (the latest fine release was 4.0.9).

Currently, the issue I have stems from that [`conda.core.package_cache.find_new_location`](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/27295f791cd8d86c6d4bebae3626f81047987c08/conda/core/package_cache.py#L143) finds `zlib` package, which is installed into a read-only location, and tries to download a new package from conda-forge into that read-only location:

```
$ conda create --yes -n _root --channel conda-forge zlib
...
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/opt/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/exceptions.py", line 479, in conda_exception_handler
        return_value = func(*args, **kwargs)
      File "/opt/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/main.py", line 145, in _main
        exit_code = args.func(args, p)
      File "/opt/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/main_create.py", line 68, in execute
        install(args, parser, 'create')
      File "/opt/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/install.py", line 422, in install
        raise CondaSystemExit('Exiting', e)
      File "/opt/conda/lib/python3.5/contextlib.py", line 77, in __exit__
        self.gen.throw(type, value, traceback)
      File "/opt/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/common.py", line 573, in json_progress_bars
        yield
      File "/opt/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/install.py", line 420, in install
        raise CondaRuntimeError('RuntimeError: %s' % e)
    conda.exceptions.CondaRuntimeError: Runtime error: RuntimeError: Runtime error: Could not open '/opt/conda/pkgs/zlib-1.2.8-3.tar.bz2.part' for writing ([Errno 13] Permission denied: '/opt/conda/pkgs/zlib-1.2.8-3.tar.bz2.part').
```

This issue is reproducable in Conda 4.1.11, 4.2.12, and current master.

Here is a Docker one-liner:

```
$ docker run --rm --user nobody continuumio/miniconda3 conda create --yes -n _root -c conda-forge zlib
```

/cc @jakirkham (I just figured that since it is related to Conda-Forge package clashing, you might be interested in this issue)

Hi
I get the following error when I try running conda.
Python seems to work ok (see attachment).

[condaerror.txt](https://github.com/conda/conda/files/1376410/condaerror.txt)

As recommended I upload the message.
This happens after I did : pip install --upgrade google-api-python-client
I do not know if the error is related to this pip install.
Best regards
G├®rard

issue_title: `conda update --all` downgrades explicitly installed packages to update dependencies
issue_body:
TLDR; Conda will downgrade explicitly installed packages in order to update implicitly installed packages (dependencies of the explicitly installed packages).

Steps to reproduce this behaviour: I have a fresh install of Miniconda3 on my system with the following in my `.condarc`:
```
channels:
  - conda-forge
  - defaults
channel_priority: false
```
After the fresh install, I run:
1. `conda update --all` (resulting in conda version 4.3.8)
2. `conda install flake8 awscli`
3. `conda update --all`

On step (3) I see:
```
>conda update --all
Fetching package metadata .............
Solving package specifications: .

Package plan for installation in environment C:\Users\laurent\Miniconda3:

The following packages will be UPDATED:

    pyflakes: 1.3.0-py35_0 conda-forge --> 1.5.0-py35_0 conda-forge

The following packages will be DOWNGRADED due to dependency conflicts:

    flake8:   3.2.1-py35_0 conda-forge --> 3.0.4-py35_0 conda-forge

Proceed ([y]/n)?
```
Here, conda chooses to update the dependency `pyflakes`, causing the explicitly installed `flake8` to receive a significant downgrade. Instead, I would prefer it to not upgrade dependencies at the cost of downgrading explicitly installed packages (unless perhaps the number of updated packages outweighs the number of downgraded ones, but that is not the case here).

issue_title: Create a RELEASE.md file to document step by step release process
issue_body:
The idea, is that anyone with git, conda basic knowledge should be able to follow these steps 1 by 1 and have a successful release of conda.

A simple example can be found for [loghub](https://github.com/spyder-ide/loghub/blob/master/RELEASE.md)

I have pooched conda packages many times due to mid-install/update failures by conda. At best it leaves the associated environment in an indeterminate state, but at worst (and this is often the case) it totally breaks the environment.

I am not the only person who has suffered this fate, and the worst case of it is when `conda` itself is eliminated from the root environment.  For 99.99% of the population this is certain death for their Anaconda installation (I'm not kidding: I bet even less than 1:10,000 people could recover from that).

The thing is all the pieces are still there in the package cache to fix up Anaconda.  I have previously done this through a complicated sequence of moving and rsyncing and reinstalling, but for individual packages that are borked in an environment (including, critically, `conda` in the root environment) I think the routine I tried out for the first time today has the kernel of a general solution:

```
pushd ~/anaconda/pkgs/conda-4.1.3-py27_0
for f in `find lib bin -type d`; do
    mkdir -p  ~/anaconda/$f
done
for f in `find lib -type f`; do 
    ln $f ~/anaconda/$f
done
for f in `cat info/has_prefix`; do
    rm ~/anaconda/$f
    cp $f ~/anaconda/$f
done
vim `cat info/has_prefix` # then manually change the shbang path, though sed could also do this
```

An experimental and (perhaps) undocumented script that somewhat more robustly implemented this inside the Anaconda distribution might come in handy.

So this issue is to make a case and record the option of doing just such a thing, even if it never comes to light.  The technique is still useful as a last ditch effort that is better than reinstall and better than rsync (IMO).

Any explanation what the solver is doing here?

`create -n test35 python=3.5 anaconda=4.1`
`conda install -n test35 matplotlib=1.5.2`

```
The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    mkl-rt:       11.1-p0            defaults

The following packages will be UPDATED:

    anaconda:     4.1.1-np111py35_0  defaults --> custom-py35_0       defaults
    freetype:     2.5.5-1            defaults --> 2.6.3-1             conda-forge
    h5py:         2.6.0-np111py35_1  defaults --> 2.6.0-np110py35_1   defaults
    jpeg:         8d-1               defaults --> 9b-0                conda-forge

The following packages will be DOWNGRADED due to dependency conflicts:

    astropy:      1.2.1-np111py35_0  defaults --> 1.1.2-np110py35_0   defaults

This is an issue tracker for conda -- the package manager.  File feature requests
for conda here, as well as bug reports about something conda has messed up.

If your issue is a bug report or feature request for:
  * a specific conda package from Anaconda ('defaults' channel):

  * a specific conda package from conda-forge:

  * anaconda.org access and service:
      ==> file at https://anaconda.org/contact/report

      ==> file at https://github.com/conda/conda-build

  * all other conda commands that start with 'conda':
      ==> please file it here!

-->

**I'm submitting a...** (I don't know whether it is a bug or feature)
  - [x] bug report
  - [x] feature request


## Current Behavior
<!-- What actually happens?
     If you want to include console output, please use "Steps to Reproduce" below. -->


### Steps to Reproduce
<!-- If the current behavior is a bug, please provide specific, minimal steps to independently reproduce.
     Include the exact conda commands that reproduce the issue and their output between the ticks below. -->
Create an environment, 'test' using Conda 4.4, open a new tab and directly activate that environment. Now try to invoke conda commands using any non shell program (lua/python/java/....) it will error out saying `conda: command not found` or `No such file or directory` since they can't `exec()` that.

## Expected Behavior
<!-- What do you think should happen? -->
The program invoking conda should not error out, or should have a way to figure out where the conda  program resides, so that it can exec the full `/path/to/conda`. Currently the only thing close to this is `$_CONDA_EXE`, but it is not documented, and also not exported, so not available to child processes and no guarantee whether it will be available in future releases.

The current work around is to first activate the base environment and then activate the non-base environment in the same shell session, which for whatever reason, retains the root environment's bin directory in the PATH.

issue_title: Conda clone does not handle symlinks consistently depending on where it resides
issue_body:
As part of the install of some packages the customer creates a symlink to a directory at the root of the current conda env.

When cloning this conda environment containing a symlink, conda resolves this symlink and then copies (or hard links) the files it finds into the cloned environment. On the other hand, if instead of creating a symlink at the root level of the conda env, they create a symlink in a subdirectory.  The clone operation just recreates the original symlink. 

```
/conda_env_path/{symlink} 
```

This copies the entire symlinked directory to the new clone environment.

```
/conda_env_path/subdir/{symlink}
```

Recreates the symlink in the new clone environment.

Why is this the case? The customers preference would be for symlinks to be recreated and not resolved/copied during cloning but we should at least be consistent on how it handles symlinks in the root as well as in a sub directory of that environment.

issue_title: Doc: need to explain the ramifications of hardlinks for lightweight envs
issue_body:
I think the documentation needs to very explicitly explain the ramifications of hard links.  If a user edits a file in an env, it will usually be modified in the package cache and other envs. There should be warnings in the documentation about what will happen if one edits files in their envs.  Most people don't consider that envs are hard links, but our implementation is bleeding through.  

I've always considered envs as disposable, so the fact that editing in an env changes global state isn't something I've thought about.

issue_title: scan() cannot raise exception in case of not all shards being successful
issue_body:
In the case of a RED status index, we do not get "failed" shards, we just get not all shards being successful e.g. JSON snippet below:

"_shards":{"total":20,"successful":7,"failed":0}

The scan() method allows a raise_on_exception parameter but this has the following issues:

1) It only raises if a shard is in the "failed" state but cannot be used to raise when not all shards were successful

2) It is only checked after yielding all successful docs, potentially meaning you scan through a huge number of docs and only get the exception at the end, when the server indicates shard status immediately in the HTTP response and so the exception could be raise immediately in principle.

3) The response data is not accessible to the caller so it is not possible to check the shard status without re-implementing the entire method.

It would be great if the functionality could include the following:

1) raise_on_error can cause an exception when not all shards are successful
2) Exception is raised immediately before yielding docs

Relevant code is linked below:

https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/blob/master/elasticsearch/helpers/__init__.py#L374-L388

issue_title: Creating document with automatic ID generation doesn't work in 5.0
issue_body:
It seems to me that a2cd22dbf6 removed the possibility to create a document using [automatic ID generation](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/docs-index_.html#_automatic_id_generation). Is that an oversight?

If yes, I'll be happy to provide a PR that restores this functionality, by changing `id` back to a keyword argument, and using `POST` if it is `None`.

issue_title: Support for Amazon AWS Hosted Elastic
issue_body:
Amazon's hosted elastic deployment requires that all requests to the service be signed using their protocol - provided we don't want to specify specific IPs in their access policy. In most production deployments, this would be the case. However, elasticsearch-py does not have the capability (or even the instrumentation) to allow the user to plugin a "Request Signer". A request signer takes all the parameters of the request and generates the additional headers that need to be added to the request.

We have locally written extensions to cope with this, alongwith a RequestSigner class that can sign elasticsearch requests appropriately to work with AWS. Given AWS' popularity and the structure of their hosted solution, it would be great if this can be supported natively in elasticsearch-py. Other hosted services might also require request signing in the future and hence generic pluggable Request Signing would be a useful addition.

[P.S. - We would like to contribute back code to do this in a generic way; as well as provide a functioning request signer that works for Amazon's hosted Elastic service]

issue_title: az vm run-command invoke produces cached output
issue_body:
---

### Environment summary

Install Method (e.g. pip, interactive script, apt-get, Docker, MSI, edge build) / CLI version (`az --version`) / OS version / Shell Type (e.g. bash, cmd.exe, Bash on Windows)  

```
Docker version of azure-cli: 
azuresdk/azure-cli-python   latest              0bd2597e121d        6 days ago          388MB

bash-4.3# az --version
azure-cli (2.0.29)

acr (2.0.22)
acs (2.0.28)
advisor (0.5.0)
appservice (0.1.29)
backup (1.0.7)
batch (3.1.11)
batchai (0.1.6)
billing (0.1.7)
cdn (0.0.13)
cloud (2.0.12)
cognitiveservices (0.1.11)
command-modules-nspkg (2.0.1)
configure (2.0.14)
consumption (0.2.2)
container (0.1.19)
core (2.0.29)
cosmosdb (0.1.19)
dla (0.0.18)
dls (0.0.19)
eventgrid (0.1.11)
eventhubs (0.1.0)
extension (0.0.10)
feedback (2.1.0)
find (0.2.8)
interactive (0.3.17)
iot (0.1.18)
keyvault (2.0.20)
lab (0.0.17)
monitor (0.1.3)
network (2.0.25)
nspkg (3.0.2)
profile (2.0.20)
rdbms (0.1.0)
redis (0.2.11)
reservations (0.1.1)
resource (2.0.25)
role (2.0.20)
servicebus (0.1.0)
servicefabric (0.0.11)
sql (2.0.23)
storage (2.0.27)
vm (2.0.28)

Python location '/usr/local/bin/python'
Extensions directory '/root/.azure/cliextensions'

Python (Linux) 3.6.3 (default, Dec 20 2017, 01:32:21) 
[GCC 5.3.0]

Legal docs and information: aka.ms/AzureCliLegal

```

When using azure cli to run a command on machine, the output for running the command twice in a row is cached and not updated to the most recent run.

Easy to reproduce with any linux machine:

```
bash-4.3# az vm run-command invoke --command-id runshellscript --name testweb --resource-group testgroup --scripts "date"
```
Run this twice in a row and you'll see the time doesn't change even though its run at different times.

Example output for both executions:
```
  "output": [
    {
      "code": "ProvisioningState/succeeded",
      "displayStatus": "Provisioning succeeded",
      "level": "Info",
      "message": "Enable succeeded: \n[stdout]\nFri Mar 16 05:11:22 UTC 2018\n\n[stderr]\n"
    }
  ]
```

I found this script gets uploaded on a clean vm to /var/lib/waagent/run-command/download/0

The script doesn't change so it doesn't seem to create a new file on output but the stdout file doesn't update either.

Related Issue happening for powershell version as well: https://github.com/Azure/azure-powershell/issues/5757

issue_title: az vm extension set: LRO polling hangs indefinitely

This is a worse manifestation of #596 

    az vm extension set -n CustomScriptForLinux --publisher Microsoft.OSTCExtensions --auto-upgrade-minor-version -g ahmet-test --vm-name ub16 --settings '{"commandToExecute":"date"}'

This command normally takes 1-2 minutes to complete...

Start this command and wait 3 seconds and start hitting ctrl+c, you won't be able to exit it.

I had to stop the process using OS X `kill` command.

issue_title: [Output] Table format does not work consistently with show commands
issue_body:
The following:
`az vm show --ids {id} -o table` works but `az vm show --ids [multiple ids] -o table` produces a table with one row full of None values. The reason is because the `simple_output_query` is expecting a single object, not an array of objects. Simply appending '[]' to the query will cause the multi-id form to succeed but the single object form to fail.

One solution would be to check the results format and wrap it in a list if it is an object. In this way the simple output query would always be written as for a list. @derekbekoe, @johanste

issue_title: `az ad sp create-for-rbac` fails even after deleting the created sp
issue_body:
### Description
It seems like `az ad sp create-for-rbac` is creating a service principal only, so after running `az ad sp delete`, deleting the sp leaves the application (which makes sense).

The problem is when deleting a sp created with create-for-rbac, then trying to run `az ad sp create-for-rbac`, the command fails unless you delete the app.

Seems this command should be `az ad app create-for-rbac` or take into account the app may still exist and simply add another sp to the existing app.

Add to route table create/update.

**Blocked** pending SDK publication

issue_title: Feature request: log likelihood of each sequence
issue_body:
Hello,

The current implementation of HMMs provides quite a few functions returning the likelihood of the data under the trained model such as `predict_proba`, `score` and `score_samples`, but lacks a function which would return the likelihood of each action sequence (instead of a likelihood per sample, or per component). This feature is essential to several applications such as sequence-based anomaly detection.

A first draft of the function could be as follows:

```
def score_samples(self, X, lengths=None)
    check_is_fitted(self, "startprob_")
    self._check()

    X = check_array(X)
    logprob = []
    for i, j in iter_from_X_lengths(X, lengths):
        framelogprob = self._compute_log_likelihood(X[i:j])
        logprob.append(self._do_forward_pass(framelogprob)[0])
    return np.array(logprob)
```

What do you think?

**Expected behavior**
Should be able to execute specs

**Actual behavior**
```
[Error - 11:20:58] Failed to read execution status information. Reason: File /Users/sswaroop/work/python/basic/.gauge/executionStatus.json doesn't exist.
[Info  - 11:20:58] Connection to server got closed. Server will restart.
```

**Steps to replicate**
* Create a new python project

* Run all specifications

Commit Hash: 7d3dc0a

Plugins

```

issue_title: Documentation for adding routes out of sync with interface
issue_body:
The `web` documentation shows the following for adding routes to the application:

```python
routes = web.RouteTableDef()

@routes.get('/')
async def index(req):
    return web.Response(text='Hello')

app = web.Application()
app.add_routes(routes)  # <--
```
For example here in the quickstart docs:
https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/master/docs/web_quickstart.rst#run-a-simple-web-server

But it seems like `Application` does not provide an `add_routes` function:

> AttributeError: 'Application' object has no attribute 'add_routes'

Instead it looks like we have to go through the app's router at `web.router.add_routes(routes)`.

Is this a recent change and should get documented properly or is this a regression in the API?

## Expected behaviour

Quickstart examples for adding routes to the app work.

## Actual behaviour

Quickstart examples for adding routes to the app does not work, has to go through app's router property.

## Steps to reproduce

Follow first steps in docs for the `aiohttp.web` package.

## Your environment

Python 3.5, latest aiohttp (2.3.10) from pip

issue_title: Allow for specifying full path to zipkin endpoint
issue_body:
Right now the zipkin address always has `api/v2/spans` appended to it, which doesn't allow for providing a full URL for the transport when the receiver requires a different URL (in our case it's a custom queue provider which then forwards the spans to proper collectors). Overriding that requires creation of a custom transport and `create()` function. 

This is somewhat related to #140

Do like this:
```
REPO_NAME={{ YOUR_REPO_NAME }}
travis encrypt -r "aio-libs/${REPO_NAME}" --api-endpoint 'https://api.travis-ci.com/'
```

Ref: https://github.com/orgs/aio-libs/teams/admins/discussions/9

issue_title: Strange behavior using a notebook from the other side of the planet
issue_body:
I'm using a notebook server that's in the US while out of the country.  The client keeps popping up a dialog box with the following message:

    The notebook file has changed on disk since the last time we opened or saved it. Do you want to overwrite the file on disk with the version open here, or load the version on disk (reload the page)?

When I'm away from the notebook for a while, it looks like quite a few of them pop up, as I have to click repeatedly to make the dialog go away (and the dimmed background becomes less dim the more I click).

There are two issues.
 
 1. The notebook on disk hasn't changed.  I suspect something in the client-side code is comparing the current time to the server-side modification time of the notebook file.  If that's the case, I think that's a bug.
 1. The dialog box seems to pop up multiple times.  I'm not a UI person, so I might be wrong about this, but it sure seems like that's what's happening.

issue_title: Kernel dies when running code twice (numba related?)
issue_body:
I've been trying to write some code using numba and I've been getting 'dead kernel' errors in Jupyter Notebook. I am using Conda, and I just did what I believe is a clean uninstall (using anaconda-clean) and reinstall of Conda, followed by a Conda update (I am using elementaryOS, so Ubuntu 16.04.2 LTS). The weird thing is that it works the first time I run the code, but the kernel dies when I run it a second time. To be specific, I run the below (pascal calculates x Choose y and fills in a 2D numpy array, and combo_to_array assigns an int to a tuple):

```
from numba import jit
import numpy as np
# fills in Pascal's triangle
# note that sectors is number of sectors in data, i.e. +1 due to HP
@jit(nopython=True)
def pascal(time,sectors):
    out=np.zeros((time+sectors,time+sectors),dtype=np.int64)
    for x in range(1,time+sectors+1):
        for y in range(1,x+1):
            product=1
            for i in range(y+1,x+1):
                product=product*i
            for i in range(1,x-y+1):
                product=product/i
            out[x][y]=product
    return out

# convert tuple (including extra HP sector) to array
@jit(nopython=True)
def combo_to_array(time,endowment,pascal):
    counter=0
    sectors=len(endowment)-1
    cumulative=0
    for idx,val in enumerate(endowment[:-1]):
        for i in range(val):
            counter+=pascal[time+sectors-1-i-cumulative-idx][sectors-idx-1]
        cumulative+=val
    return int(counter)
```

then I run 
```
sectors=3
time=5
choose=pascal(time,sectors+1)
print('(4,1,0,0) maps to '+str(combo_to_array(time,(4,1,0,0),choose)))
```
and get the correct output of

> (4,1,0,0) maps to 54


However, if I run

```
sectors=3
time=5
choose=pascal(time,sectors+1)
print('(4,1,0,0) maps to '+str(combo_to_array(time,(4,1,0,0),choose)))
```

issue_title: CSS: Not enough space before bullet lists
issue_body:
Probably the existing rule from `renderedhtml.less` should be slightly altered:

```css
.rendered_html ul:not(.list-inline), .rendered_html ol:not(.list-inline) {
    margin: 1em 2em 0em 2em;
    padding-left: 0px;
}
```

This is an example where the problem is obvious:

```markdown
### Title
* one
* two
```

BTW, this is how it looks on Github:

### Title
* one
* two

issue_title: Linking Jupyter kernelspec to Anaconda Python
issue_body:
I've actually asked this [question](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/46551200/changing-jupyter-kernelspec-to-point-to-anaconda-python) at Stack Overflow, but thought this might be a more appropriate venue. Apologies if this is inappropriate.

So, here's my issue, slightly elaborated for this more specific audience.

I'm using Anaconda to manage both Python and Jupyter. That is:

```
>> which python
>> /home/.../software/anaconda3/bin/python
```
and

```
>> which jupyter
>> /home/.../software/anaconda3/bin/jupyter
```

But Jupyter's python kernel seems to be pointing to a system version of Python rather than my local version through Anaconda, since the `sys.path` is different in a Jupyter Python 3 notebook. Also, `jupyter kernelspec list` gives the following:

```
 Available kernels:
   ir         /usr/local/share/jupyter/kernels/ir
   matlab     /usr/local/share/jupyter/kernels/matlab
   python3    /usr/local/share/jupyter/kernels/python3
```
This doesn't seem altogether surprising since the [docs](https://media.readthedocs.org/pdf/jupyter/latest/jupyter.pdf) say in section 1.5.5:

> By default, kernel specs will go in a system-wide location (e.g. /usr/local/share/jupyter/kernels). If doing a --user install, the kernel specs will go in the JUPYTER_DATA_DIR location.

For personal sanity and organization, I want the version of Python that I use in the command line to be the same that is accessed in Jupyter, which as a reminder was installed through Anaconda for my user account on the computer. As a result, I *think* that what I should do is change my jupyter kernelspec list for python3 so that it points to my desired Anaconda python version, i.e. `/home/.../software/anaconda3/bin/python`. My questions are: 1) is that indeed the best solution for my stated preferences, 2) is there any chance that the command `jupyter kernelspec list` is showing kernels for a globally installed version jupyter, rather than my own version, and 3) how do I actually change my jupyter kernelspec entry for python3? Not sure if this will come up, but I want to clarify that I don't want to be using virtual environments--I want the default to be same version of Python across both the command line and Jupyter.

issue_title: Contributing dev docs: jupyter instances and verification of installation
issue_body:
I was working on #2460 when I realized I wasn't running Jupyter in dev mode, and I wasn't sure how to verify my dev installation. [This](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/issues/2460#issuecomment-378172208) was written to me in the issue. The comment mentioned that I should uninstall other instances of Jupyter and told me how to verify the installation. Perhaps we could include the following in the contributing docs:

1. Uninstalling other instances of Jupyter
2. How to verify the installation

Not sure if (1) was an isolated incident, but perhaps (2) might be beneficiary to new contributors?

W7907 	%s Duplicate xml field "%s" in lines %s 	duplicate-xml-fields
issue_body:
This error should be reduced to informational or ignored in demo data xml files.  It is entirely legitimate and occasionally necessary to have duplicate xml-ids,  even if 99% of the time it is an error.  

Sometimes, such as when dealing with products, product templates and attributes you need to create circular references in order to trigger ORM create functions correctly and get around required fields of each model.

issue_title: Fix for #1 broke ability to have cached querylists
issue_body: See comment on #1 - because querysets are all forced to be re-evaluated, it's now impossible to cache the DB queries which is essential for some users (e.g. me :) )

issue_title: Entering multiple A records for same IP
issue_body:
I was asked to open an issue here (coming from Infoblox community forums). Brief overview:

I'm messing around with the Python Infoblox API and having difficulty getting a records with the same IP address but different domain names to push to the database.
 
For instance:
 
1.1.1.1 test.com
1.1.1.1 test2.com
 
currently, only the first entry will be entered into the database. Is there any way to force Infoblox to add another A record instead of just updating or ignoring if it sees a matching IP already exists?


`from netaddr import *
import logging
import certifi
import urllib3
import time
import requests
import json
import getpass
import sys
import os
import fnmatch
import re
import validators
import infoblox_client # Is this redundant with below?
from infoblox_client import connector
from infoblox_client import objects

# These are for the infoblox lab test environment
infoblox_IP = 'x.x.x.x' 
infoblox_username = 'username'
infoblox_password = 'password'
infoblox_zone = 'any_domain'
opts = {'host': infoblox_IP, 'username': infoblox_username, 'password': infoblox_password}
conn = connector.Connector(opts)
wapi_url = 'https://infoblox-lab-url/wapi/v2.2.2/'


def aRecord():
    x = 0
    a_record_ips = []
    a_record_names = []
    print('''
        ***************************************************************************************
        **** NOTE: After entering the final record addition, just press [ENTER] to proceed ****
        ***************************************************************************************
        ''')
    while True:
        print('What is the IP address of the A record?')
        record_IP = input('> ')
        if record_IP == '':
            if len(a_record_ips) < 1:
                print('''
                    Returning to Main Menu!
                    ''')
            elif len(a_record_ips) == len(a_record_names):
                aRecordAdd(a_record_ips, a_record_names)
            else:
                # raise an error explaining not enough names were added
                aRecord()
        else:
            a_record_ips.append(record_IP)
        while True:
            print('What is the domain name of the A record?')
            record_name = input('> ') + infoblox_zone
            if record_name == '':
                continue    # Can't enter an IP without a domain name ;)
            else:
                a_record_names.append(record_name)
                break

def aRecordAdd(ips,names):
    os.system('cls')
    print('''
        Please confirm the addition of the following record(s):
        ''')
    x = 0
    while x < len(ips):
        print(str(x + 1) + '. ' + ips[x] + ' - ' + names[x])
        x += 1
    user_choice = input('> ')
    if user_choice == '':
        y = 0
        while y < len(ips):
            newRecord = objects.ARecordBase.create(conn, check_if_exits=False, ip=ips[y], name=names[y])
            y += 1
        print('''
            %s Records have been added
            ''' % (len(ips)))
    else:
        print('')`

Thank you

But I keep having weird errors:

    assert self._write_buffer_size >= 0
AssertionError

```
and then
```
ERROR:nsq.client:[127.0.0.1:4150:athena.Test123:async] ERROR: SendError: failed to send FIN b'0c18c9d08fd9d001' (Stream is closed)
```

My code is as follows:
I use a `cleanup_loop` to check if thread is dead and make the message `finish()`
```
consume_tasks = []

def consume_func(content):
    # do custom stuff with content
    return

def async_handler(message):
    global consume_tasks
    message.enable_async()

    job = threading.Thread(
        target=consume_func,
        args=(message.body.decode(),)
    )
    consume_tasks.append({
        'message': message,
        'job': job,
    })
    job.start()

def cleanup_loop():
    global consume_tasks
    while True:
        unfinished_tasks = []
        for task in consume_tasks:
            if not task.get('job').is_alive():
                task.get('message').finish()
            else:
                unfinished_tasks.append(task)
        consume_tasks = unfinished_tasks
        time.sleep(0.1)

reader = nsq.Reader(message_handler=handler, lookupd_http_addresses='',
                    topic='Test123', channel='async', max_in_flight=10)
threading.Thread(target=cleanup_loop).start()
nsq.run()

```

issue_title: Generating DLLs in different folders breaks VS Enterprise Architecture
issue_body:
It appears the layer diagram feature under the Architecture menu on Visual Studio Enterprise expects all DLLs to be available under the `bin/` folder of each project after compilation. However, with .NET Core they seem to be generated under other folders, for example `bin/Debug/netcoreapp1.0/` or `bin/Debug/netstandard1.6/`. See [screenshot]
(https://www.dropbox.com/s/f14kcz8ty3nax3m/dll-error.png?dl=0).

As a side note, the layer diagram feature also seems to break when setting a different `buildOptions.outputName` in `project.json`.

I am using Visual Studio 2015 update 3.

issue_title: Support creating a new certificate without making it "live"
issue_body:
My server's DNS zone is secured via DNSSEC and TLSA, so I cannot just create a new certificate and use it. I have to create it, put its fingerprint into the zone, wait for 24h until that new TLSA record propagated, and only *then* actually make the certificate live.

So far, I haven't figured out a good way to do this with the letsencrypt client.

issue_title: letsencrypt-auto depends on sudo / no support for PREFIX?
issue_body:
(Or, "Dude, where's my sudo?")

As it stands, if you don't have `sudo`, you're toasted. The default in Debian is to not install `sudo` for bare-metal installations. As such, a Debian Jesse/stable minimalist installation doesn't have `sudo`, because there's a password set for `root`.

Should `letsencrypt-auto` depend on sudo? Or just say "Run me as root"? Or, optionally list the packages that need to be installed?

For completeness, this is the entire output from `letsencrypt-auto`:

```
indrora@zaibatsutel:~/letsencrypt$ ./letsencrypt-auto 
Bootstrapping dependencies for Debian-based OSes...
./letsencrypt-auto: 40: ./letsencrypt-auto: sudo: not found
```

Alternately, would a message of "sudo isn't there, using su -c '...'" be appropriate?

issue_title: Create tests needed to achieve 100% code coverage for __init__.py
monitor congestion on brokers with stats_cb/position/commited?
issue_body:
I have a consumer app and a producer app, and they will be exchanging possibly high-volume traffic over a kafka cluster. I don't get to put any of my own code on the cluster, it is just a generic kafka cluster. It would be interesting to me to see if the consumer is falling behind, and data was building up on the cluster from within my code (either producer or consumer). Is there a way to do this? On the consumer, I see that I can do consumer.position() and consumer.committed(), but their outputs don't look very interesting.

The stats_cb gives me a big blob of JSON, but I don't know how to interpret it. I can see each partition, and several different *_offset stats: hi_offset, next_offset, app_offset, eof_offset, and stored_offset. Sometimes these are all the same, some of them are -1001 (what does this mean?), and sometimes some are lower than others. When this is the case, is the difference an indication of how behind the consumer is? It seems that app_offset is always lower than or equal to hi_offset and next_offset, while eof_offset can be as high as hi_offset or lower than it.

Are the per-broker "throttle" stats what I want?

in general, is there a cheat sheet to help me interpret the stats_cb JSON blob? Barring that, is there a way to see what I want, that is, to determine if we are producing faster than we are consuming?

[workflow genome-resequencing.cwl] start

issue_title: Dynamic Symbology in the Layer Selector?
issue_body:
Hi Folium team,

This is a great package.  I am amazed at how elegant it is, even with medium-sized data sets (e.g., I have a 120MB html file that pans and zooms quickly).

I wanted to ask if you plan to add support for dynamic symbology (or if it's already there and I somehow missed it) or for a folium server?  Let's take a US river polyline file, for example.  I'd like to be able to select in the layer view to not just turn it on or off, but also to symbolize it based on the depth of the riverbed, or, alternatively, the temperature of the water.  Just an example.  I wanted to ask if this type of functionality is possible?  It would open up a world of possibilities, even more than what is currently possible.

I am currently using bokeh for this type of functionality.  It is not as elegant and admittedly not designed for web mapping, but it does offer this type of streaming and changes to symbology.

Finally, I read the contributing guidelines.  I am happy to help in any way that I can.  I am relatively new to python, but I am going through dozens of real-life data science and data viz case studies every week.

issue_title: A bug in the abstract property in medlinecitation.py
issue_body:
Hello.
I have been deeply impressed by the convenience provided by eutils package and appreciate your contribution!
In the meantime, I may find a bug in medlinecitation.py:
`        return self._xml_root.findtext('Article/Abstract/AbstractText')`
(https://github.com/biocommons/eutils/blob/master/eutils/xmlfacades/medlinecitation.py#L15)

The code not seems to extract full text of an abstract in the case where there are multi AbstractText elements, (e.g. 22351513[pmid]).
I think if you modify the code below, it probably works well:
`        return '\n'.join([at.text for at in self._xml_root.findall('Article/Abstract/AbstractText')])`

NCBI apparently made a subtle change to eutilities that breaks sequence fetching by seqfetcher. Previously, fetching NP sequences from nucleotide worked fine; it stopped working around Feb 1, 2017. 

This change will break hgvs validation for folks not using seqrepo, so the fix is urgent.

issue_title: Feature Request: built-in validator that object already exists
issue_body:
Some of my API's let people create new objects, and they have to specify a foreign key. Right now I have some custom validators that check the database to verify that the foreign relationship object exists, but this strikes me as something that would be useful as a built-in validator for  `marshmallow-sqlalchemy`

It's somewhat straightforward, although there's an implementation question whether to use session.query(foreign object).get(pk id) or something a little more fancy like: 

    if not db.session.query(db.exists([GearCategory.id]).where(GearCategory.id == category_id)).scalar():
       raise Exception("Gear Category ID [%s] does not exist" % category_id)

The `get()` will be far faster when the object is already in the session, but slower if it has to go check the db. Although if you go with the `get()`, I suggest using a `load_only('id')` query option for speed reasons.

The list of lists that is returned by ```serial.tools.list_ports.comports()``` is inflexible and prone to error.

Code and responses are from Python 3.4.3 running on OS X

For example in this invocation of ```comports()``` I get back a reply of

```python
[['/dev/cu.Bluetooth-Incoming-Port', 'n/a', 'n/a'],
 ['/dev/cu.Bluetooth-Modem', 'n/a', 'n/a'],
 ['/dev/cu.usbserial', 'USB-Serial Controller', 'USB VID:PID=67b:2303 SNR=None']]
```

Now I can try matching with a grep on element 2 of each possible port but this is fragile across operating systems.  On OS X we have some os specific USB properties such as location ID and if the device is considered a modem or a plain RS232 serial stream.

Location ID is a 32bit number that maps to a specific location on the USB bus that will not change if the hub topology is not changed upstream of the device.  i.e. if you leave your USB bus alone the location ID will not change.  This is useful for working with USB micro controllers that switch modes.  e.g. from a USB-CDC interface you could issue a command to your part to enter a USB-DFU mode. If you knew your location ID before, you could attach to a DFU interface at that location ID.

As different systems have different serial port metadata, I propose a list of dictionaries to be returned by ```comports()```.

the above example would become

```python
[
  {'device': '/dev/cu.Bluetooth-Incoming-Port'},
  {'device': '/dev/cu.Bluetooth-Modem'},
  {'device': '/dev/cu.usbserial',
    'USB PID': 8963,
    'USB VID': 1659, 
    'name': 'USB-Serial Controller',
    'location_id': 0xfa130000,
  },
]
```
Clearly this dictionary only includes keys that are populated. We could use the  ```collections.DefaultDict``` to default non existent keys to ```None``` for convenience.

Alternatively we could use a named tuple and make the return more static.

Function name could change to maintain compatibly.
Thoughts?

http://pythonwheels.com/

Right now, only the `tar.gz` file is being distributed on PyPI for [coreapi](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/coreapi), which isn't _that much_ of an issue, but it does add some time to installing the package.

This package should be compatible with the Wheel format, considering it doesn't appear to have any C dependencies and it is compatible with both Python 2 and 3. As a result, you should only need to generate a universal wheel and then everyone (on all systems) will get the ability to install coreapi with just the wheel, without having to do any extra work.

---

Note that this also applies to all of the other Core API modules which are published as Python packages.

issue_title: Strange regression between 4.5.0 and current master (4.5.1)
issue_body:
We are using z3 in a concolic testing tool, called [CutEr](https://github.com/aggelgian/cuter/) and are heavily relying on it -- thanks for all your excellent work, by the way!

We have noticed various regressions between v4.5.0, which we use on Travis, and the current `master` branch, which we use for local testing on our own machines (and so that we can notice problems before it is too late).

One of them, perhaps the simplest one is shown below:
```sh
$ z3 -version
Z3 version 4.5.0 - 64 bit
$ z3 -smt2 -T:2 cuter1.smt2 
sat
$ z3 -smt2 -T:2 cuter2.smt2
sat

$  z3 -version
Z3 version 4.5.1 - 64 bit
$ z3 -smt2 -T:2 cuter1.smt2 
unknown
$ z3 -smt2 -T:2 cuter2.smt2
sat
```

Files can be [found here](https://gist.github.com/aggelgian/61d82ff30a6d3700dee060d85dc020c2).  Seems it's not possible to attach `.smt2` files on GitHub.

Thanks to @aggelgian for minimizing the examples.

PS. There are various other regressions between v4.5.0 and master, but we'll leave these for after this one is fixed.

(declare-const s String)

(assert (= s "F"))

issue_title: Question about apply_momentum function in Lasagne
issue_body:
```
def apply_momentum(updates, params=None, momentum=0.9):
    """Returns a modified update dictionary including momentum

    Generates update expressions of the form:

    * ``velocity := momentum * velocity + updates[param] - param``
    * ``param := param + velocity``

    Parameters
    ----------
    updates : OrderedDict
        A dictionary mapping parameters to update expressions
    params : iterable of shared variables, optional
        The variables to apply momentum to. If omitted, will apply
        momentum to all `updates.keys()`.
    momentum : float or symbolic scalar, optional
        The amount of momentum to apply. Higher momentum results in
        smoothing over more update steps. Defaults to 0.9.

    Returns
    -------
    OrderedDict
        A copy of `updates` with momentum updates for all `params`.

    Notes
    -----
    Higher momentum also results in larger update steps. To counter that,
    you can optionally scale your learning rate by `1 - momentum`.

    See Also
    --------
    momentum : Shortcut applying momentum to SGD updates
    """
    if params is None:
        params = updates.keys()
    updates = OrderedDict(updates)

    for param in params:
        value = param.get_value(borrow=True)
        velocity = theano.shared(np.zeros(value.shape, dtype=value.dtype),
                                 broadcastable=param.broadcastable)
        x = momentum * velocity + updates[param]
        updates[velocity] = x - param
        updates[param] = x

    return updates
```
In 
```
for param in params:
        value = param.get_value(borrow=True)
        velocity = theano.shared(np.zeros(value.shape, dtype=value.dtype),
                                 broadcastable=param.broadcastable)
        x = momentum * velocity + updates[param]
        updates[velocity] = x - param
        updates[param] = x
```
My question is about the velocity variable, In my opinion, every param should have a distinct velocity,
Is it legal that all of them have the same name (in this case, velocity)?
How theano distinguish between them...
Sorry if I ask a naive question.

Why is the learning rate not part of the formula presented in the documentation for  nesterov_momentum:
```
velocity := momentum * velocity + updates[param] - param
param := param + momentum * velocity + updates[param] - param
```
Looking at the code it looks like it is used in the same way as momentum.

issue_title: Raise a warning when a keyword passed to `get_output()` is unused
issue_body:
`lasagne.layers.get_output()` can be called with keyword arguments, which are passed to all traversed layers. This makes it possible to control layers with multiple behaviours (e.g. dropout, which can be turned on or off, or batch normalization, which can use minibatch statistics or aggregated statistics).

The most common use case for this is the `deterministic` keyword, which is used to turn off any regularizing noise in the net at test time (i.e. dropout).

The handling of these keywords is implemented in the `get_output_for()` method of each `Layer` class, which is required to take a catchall `**kwargs` argument to absorb any keywords that the layer doesn't recognize.

One issue with this is that a `Layer` instance might unintentionally ignore a certain keyword, and this is basically a silent failure. If you misspell `deterministic` as `determinstic` for example, all layers will ignore it and no warning or exception will be raised.

This is not very user friendly. Recently I had a brief discussion about this issue on this reddit thread: https://www.reddit.com/r/MachineLearning/comments/3xom8a/discussion_what_would_you_like_to_see_in_a/cy6hqg8

One of the developers of climin ( https://github.com/BRML/climin ) chimed in and pointed me to their solution for this issue: https://github.com/BRML/climin/blob/master/climin/util.py#L140-L151

Basically, check the called function's argument specification through introspection, and see if any arguments were provided that aren't listed there. If so, raise a warning.

I think such a solution may be possible for Lasagne as well. We could have `get_output()` inspect the argument specification for all `get_output_for()` calls it makes, and keep track of the set of all accepted keyword arguments across all layers. Once the graph is traversed, we can check whether any of the provided keyword arguments are not in this set.

As far as I can tell this would only require modifications in `get_output()` itself. It would complicate the code somewhat, but not excessively so, and I think the usability benefit should not be underestimated.

There is one big caveat: not all `get_output_for()` implementations specify all keywords arguments they accept in the method signature. Some implementations work directly with the `kwargs` dictionary, most importantly the new batch normalization implementation (#467).

So we'd have to figure out a workaround for that. The simplest approach would be to always have all accepted keyword arguments in the method signature, but I don't know if that is desirable. Another solution would be to provide an alternative way for such methods to 'declare' which keyword arguments they understand, but I think that might get a little too complicated.

I might try whipping up a PR for this, but before I do that we should probably figure out what to do with accepted keyword arguments that are not explicitly declared in the method signature.

issue_title: Should not assume that matplotlib is available
issue_body:
We currently pass `--matplotlib=inline` when starting the kernel, to force figures to appear inline. However, this means that if `matplotlib` is not installed in the environment where the kernel runs (which may not be the same as the one nbval is running in), the kernel will fail to start.

From comments on #6.

pyfilesystem2 requires six version `1.10.*` in `setup.py`:

    "six~=1.10.0"

Is this a hard requirement? Version 1.11.0 of six has just been released and we've been hitting the following error ever since, when setting up our testing infrastructure:

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File ".tox/py34/lib/python3.4/site-packages/_pytest/vendored_packages/pluggy.py", line 510, in load_setuptools_entrypoints
    plugin = ep.load()
  File ".tox/py34/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2404, in load
    self.require(*args, **kwargs)
  File ".tox/py34/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 2427, in require
    items = working_set.resolve(reqs, env, installer, extras=self.extras)
  File ".tox/py34/lib/python3.4/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 872, in resolve

pkg_resources.ContextualVersionConflict: (six 1.11.0 (.tox/py34/lib/python3.4/site-packages), Requirement.parse('six~=1.10.0'), {'fs'})

I'm not able to currently pinpoint the issue (to provide you with a test), but I can fix the problem by pinning six to `1.10.0`. Could the requirement maybe be changed to `"six>=1.10.0"`?

issue_title: Programmatically find all the time series in the NWB file
issue_body:
Hello everyone.
I've been using your API to develop my [GSoC project](https://summerofcode.withgoogle.com/projects/#6057925982814208) and one of my tasks was to find a way to programmatically find all the time series present in any NWB 2.x file.
I've tried to develop this as follows:
``` python
def _get_data_interfaces(self, node):
        """Given a NWBHDF5IO returns all the data_interfaces objects presents on it."""
        data_interfaces_list = []
        for child in node.children:
            if isinstance(child, NWBDataInterface):
                data_interfaces_list.append(child)
            data_interfaces_list += self._get_data_interfaces(child)
       return data_interfaces_list

def _get_timeseries(self):
        """Given all the nwb_data_interfaces returns all of those that are timeseries objects."""
        time_series_list = []
        for data_interface in self.nwb_data_interfaces_list:
            if isinstance(data_interface, TimeSeries):
                time_series_list.append(data_interface)
return time_series_list
```
I believe this returns all the [TimeSeries objects](https://pynwb.readthedocs.io/en/latest/overview_nwbfile.html#timeseries), then I simply run over each one of them to get mono dimensional time series easily plottable:
``` python
def get_mono_dimensional_timeseries(self, values):
        """Given a timeseries object returns all mono dimensional timeseries presents on it."""
        mono_time_series_list = []
        if isinstance(values, collections.Iterable):
            try:
                data = [float(i) for i in values]
                mono_time_series_list.append(data)
            except:
                for inner_list in values:
                    mono_time_series_list += self.get_mono_dimensional_timeseries(inner_list)
return mono_time_series_list
```

I would like to get your opinion on this. 
I wasn't able to find this feature in your API. Do you think it has any value? Would you do this any other way? 
I understand that this is not a typical issue, so apologies in advance if it should be asked elsewhere. :pray:

**Steps to reproduce**:
1. Create a ``MoneyModel`` with ``MoneyField``;
2. Create a new model with ``MoneyField`` during the test or just in a runtime;
3. ``MoneyModel.objects`` is a Django's ``Manager`` instance.

**Expected behaviour**:
``MoneyModel.objects`` should be a ``MoneyManager`` instance.

The problem is, that ``Options.managers`` is a ``cached_property``, which is populated from ``Options.local_managers`` but we don't update this list and when the cache is cleared we have an unpatched manager again.

Cache is cleared when ``registry.Apps.register_model`` is called, which calls ``registry.Apps.clear_cache``, that leads to ``Options._expire_cache`` calls for every registered model.

issue_title: Deadended states lost when executing part of a binary
issue_body:

In case we skip part of a binary and start our simulation manager at a specific function (usually `main`), when we reach the end of this function, the return site is symbolic, thus the warning `Exit state has over 257 possible solutions. Likely unconstrained; skipping.`. This puts the new state into `unconstrained_successors`, however, imho, because of these lines in `manager.py`, we lost the state (it is no more in the `simgr.stashes`):
```
    if not active and not unconstrained and orig:
      always_present['deadended'].append(orig)
```

Hello,
The anonymous function 'qualify' mishandles namespaces. Indeed, defined as such:
`qualify = lambda tag, ns=BASE_NS_1_0: tag if ns is None else "{%s}%s" % (ns, tag)`

It means: if the element contains no namespace, the namespace defaults to the base NETCONF.

This definition does not cover the case where the root has a namespace and many children, but one of them has no namespace.
As suggested @plajjan (https://github.com/ncclient/ncclient/issues/176), it would be better that:

- An Element inherits its parent's namespace, if its parent has one
- Or the base NETCONF, if its parent has none.

Thanks,
Ariel

issue_title: mpsyt should reset comments current page when switching to comments of another video
issue_body:
How to reproduce:

1. See comments for a video with multiple comment pages, e.g. `c 1`
1. Press `n` some times to see next comment pages
1. See comments for another video that has **less** comment pages, e.g. `c 3`

Expected result: get the first page of comments for video 3

Actual result: a blank screen; but if you press `p` several times, you finally get comments for video 3.

It seems that mpsyt remembers the current comment page from video 1, and displays the same comment page for video 3, but since this has less comments, you get a blank page.
Sometimes I listen to a song and forget the name or search terms I used to find it. It would be nice if there was a persistent history that I could search through to try and find it again.

issue_title: Return back support for webtest positional args
issue_body:
Positional arguments were removed in #48 PR.

Webtest documentation uses `params` positional argument, for example:

```python
>>> resp = app.post_json('/resource/', dict(id=1, value='value'))
```
https://docs.pylonsproject.org/projects/webtest/en/latest/testapp.html#making-json-requests

But this does not work with django-webtest.

I'd suggest, to add `*args`, in order to return positional arguments back and make method signatures exactly as they are in webtest.

And anyway changes in #48 are backwards incompatible without any good reason.

issue_title: Using provisioning_uri occaisionally mixes up secret & issuer
issue_body:
I've noticed that upon generating a new TOTP with provisioning_uri (for Google) the string occasionally switches the secret  & issuer causing the QR generation to break (with qrious).

Working with qrious:
`otpauth://totp/Company:name%domain.com?secret=1234ABCD5678EFGH&issuer=Company`

Not Working with qrious:
`otpauth://totp/Company:name%domain.com?issuer=Company&secret=1234ABCD5678EFGH`

Should this be shared with the people working on qrious, is the format not important?

### Version of the SDK being used

2.0.0

### Expected Behavior

Should be able to specify a valid GraphAPI version (currently, that's 2.9), and get a proper `GraphAPI` instance:

```python
> api = GraphAPI(auth_token, version='2.9')
#yay!
```

### Actual Behavior

```python
> api = GraphAPI(auth_token, version='2.9')
GraphAPIError: Valid API versions are '2.1', '2.2', '2.3', '2.4', '2.5', '2.6', '2.7'
```

issue_title: Version number should be in the following format: #.# (e.g. 2.0).
issue_body:
### Version of the SDK being used
facebook-sdk-2.0.0

### Expected Behavior
Able to initiate a Graph object for version 2.10 of the API

### Actual Behavior
facebook.GraphAPIError:  Version number should be in the following format: #.# (e.g. 2.0).

### Steps to Reproduce
import facebook

graph = facebook.GraphAPI(access_token='xx', version='2.10')



Looks like the regexp to check the version number needs to be updated to support #.## instead of just #.#.

issue_title: Allow separate schema definition & implementation (and regularize graphene implementation)
issue_body:
I would like to separate my Graphene schema definition from its implementation. 

* By "definition" I mean the names of my object types, their fields, and custom scalar types.
* By "implementation" I mean the `resolve_*` functions and `mutate` functions.

(Why do I want to do this? Lots of reasons. First, it's super nice to be able to reference all the types and documentation in one place -- putting code in there too makes it far less readable. Second, the implementation requires fancy imports, like accessing my database, that the definition doesn't -- it's nice to be able to import the schema without being forced to import all the database code.)

Currently Graphene makes this quite a challenge.

Example of what I want to do --

(in graphene_models.py:)
```py
class User(ObjectType):
    name = String()

class Signup(Mutation):
    class Arguments:
        name = String()
    id = ID()
```

(in graphene_impl.py:)
```py
@resolver_for(User, 'name')
def resolve_user_name(self, info):
    return 'Cam Newton'

@mutator_for(Signup)
def mutate_signup(self, info, name):
    # do something fancy with the db
    return Signup(id=...)
```

As things stand today, I can do this, but it's terribly awkward:
* Write the `resolver_for` decorator to monkeypatch the function into, e.g., `User.resolve_name`. This only works if I delay the `Schema` construction till after the implementation module is imported, because it's the TypeMap's job to figure out the resolve functions for the fields and that happens at schema construction time. 
* For mutations, I had to look at the resolveinfo's field name and use that to figure out what class's resolver was trying to be executed, and then look for a mutator on that class. (This because the Mutation base class statically binds the mutate method at class creation time, so it's not overridable.)

I'm mainly curious about whether people agree that having the ability to separate declaration and implementation is desirable, and if so, whether there are any better ways to do it than this, or if there are any plans to improve the situation.

Thanks for your consideration and thanks for writing a very nice piece of software!

For a `ConnectionField` we can pass in a a custom edge as so: `addresses=relay.ConnectionField(Address, connection_type=AddressType, edge_type=AddressTypeEdge)`. But when the custom edge has some additional fields it seems there is no way for these to be set, as this line: https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-relay-py/blob/master/graphql_relay/connection/arrayconnection.py#L46 only ever tries to set `node` and `cursor`. It would be great to have support to resolve some custom fields on a custom edge.

I might look into a fix myself tomorrow since I need that for one of our implementations.

```python
class ModelName(Whatever, models.Model):

        self.full_clean()
        super().save(*args, **kwargs)
```

 to your models but for me it seems like something the library should take care of.

On my local computer I use django server for **same project**.  On my local computer preinstalled Pillow 3.1.2. 
Also I use sorl-thumbnail 12.3 on both.

My local computer project perfect works. Without problems.

But on my virtual server I see errors from 2010 year))) https://github.com/mariocesar/sorl-thumbnail/issues/3

issue_title: How are threads bound to physical cores in TensorFlow runtime and inter/intra threadpools?
issue_body:
Hi all,

1. If one CPU has 8 cores and each core has two threads, does TF runtime use the OS threads scheduler to match the logical cores and threads to the physical cores and threads? Or does it has its own policy to match the threads and cores? Does TF only recognize cores and but not the whole device(CPU) like the system does? (The OS only knows the number/id of cores and sockets and doesn't know how many CPU devices) Does the "**device**" in TF correspond to the physical **core**, e.g.,  TF recognizes 8 devices in the above CPU? 

2. I found out that there are inter/intra **global** and **local** threadpools in /tensorflow/core/common_runtime/local_device.cc and /tensorflow/core/common_runtime/dirrect_session.cc. Does that mean each device (or core?) have its own local inter/intra threadpool and there is only one inter/intra global threadpool in the whole TF runtime?

Thank you for your time.

issue_title: OpenThread settings seems to miss first page
issue_body:
Hi,

FTD node creates a Thread network and becomes Leader, it stores networkdata (settings) in internal memory.
Then if node resets and node tries to restore previous stored networkdata.

At the line:
https://github.com/openthread/openthread/blob/master/examples/platforms/utils/settings.cpp#L289

Wee see that we start by checking second page but what I can see it that networkdata are stored in first page. 
Isn't it a implementation bug?
Replacing
```
        sSettingsBaseAddress += settingsSize * index; 
        utilsFlashRead(sSettingsBaseAddress, (uint8_t *)(&blockFlag), sizeof(blockFlag));
```
By:
```
        utilsFlashRead(sSettingsBaseAddress, (uint8_t *)(&blockFlag), sizeof(blockFlag));
        sSettingsBaseAddress += settingsSize * index; 
```
Makes restore work!

issue_title: Build OpenThread code base for Host system.
issue_body:
A host system, one that communicates to an OpenThread NCP over a bus (UART, SPI), may have need for a modest portion of the OpenThread libraries.  These would include various header files {openthread-types.h, spinel.h, hdlc.hpp}  along with libraries that can be used to unpack spinel frames {spinel.c, hdlc.cpp}.  

The request would be to configure Openthread to build for this alternative environment.  In the future one might add additional modules that would further ease the porting/development effort of a host system.

The OpenThread project currently uses [astyle](http://astyle.sourceforge.net/) to automatically format code and provide baseline enforcement of the OpenThread code style.  In general, I've found astyle to be fairly limited in its flexibility.

This is a proposal to switch to [uncrustify](https://github.com/uncrustify/uncrustify).  For better (or worse), uncrustify supports many more options than astyle.  In short, astyle allows automated formatting and enforcement of more code style aspects, including:

- Left-aligned variable declarations.
- Aligned assignment operators.
- Left-aligned end-of-line comments.
- ... and many more.

issue_title: Prioritization of MLE messages over data and handling of child re-attach
issue_body:
Summarizing two features: 

- Prioritize MLE traffic over data packet. This is particularly important for MLE "Child Id Response" message. 

- Current implementation removes the child/neighbor when we recive a "Parent Requst" from it (child tries to re-attahc). This in turn causes all queued data packets for the node to be cleared. It'd be better to keep all the data packets  but clear out the queued MLE packets.

issue_title: Made a tool to rename columns, want to integrate it
issue_body:
Example: I often have to export data from MongoDB where columns are extracted from an hierarchy, and have weird names.

    mongoexport -d my_db -c my_collection --csv -f id,geopos.coordinates.0,geopos.coordinates.1,address > data.csv

There's no direct way in csvkit to rename those columns, only remove them completely or write a pipe, something like `echo "column1,column2" > my_file.csv && head +2 >> my_file.csv`.

I suggest to make a tool or an option to rename columns.

 * Option 1: a parameter to `csvcut`:

    csvcut source.csv --rename-columns geopos.coordinates.0:x,geopos.coornidates.1:y > dest.csv

 * Option 2: a separate tool:

    csvrename source.csv -c geopos.coordinates.0:x,geopos.coornidates.1:y > dest.csv

I have a working version of the latter in my repo. Please write what you think of this.

In common.py, the function base64url_encode() seems to be a convenience function that accepts both bytes and unicode-strings, utf8-encodes the latter, base64URLsafe encodes the bytes, strips any padding of trailing "=", and utf8-decodes the result to a unicode-string, which is returned.

However, base64 encoding should return bytes...

I can understand that you want a convenience function that returns a unicode-string such that you can easily assign it to a python object or attribute for json encoding later, but maybe rename the function to base64url_encode2ustr() or something like that to differentiate it from pure base64 encoding.

(sorry to be pedantic about this, but all this bytes vs str vs unicode in python2 vs python3 with all the inconsistent unicode/b64/json-encoding is so f#$%^ed up that keeping it clear seems to be the only way to keep ones sanity ;-) )

issue_title: API changes for the logger in Flask 1.0.0+
issue_body:
When upgrading to FLASK v1.0.0+ I get this error:

```bash
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "PATH/lib/python3.6/site-packages/flask/_compat.py", line 35, in reraise
    raise value
  File "PATH/app.py", line 40, in <module>
    talisker.flask.register(app)
  File "PATH/lib/python3.6/site-packages/talisker/flask.py", line 62, in register
    app._logger = logging.getLogger(app.logger_name)
AttributeError: 'Flask' object has no attribute 'logger_name'
```

From [changelog](http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/1.0/changelog/):
> Flask.logger has been simplified. LOGGER_NAME and LOGGER_HANDLER_POLICY config was removed. The logger is always named flask.app. The level is only set on first access, it doesnŌĆÖt check Flask.debug each time. Only one format is used, not different ones depending on Flask.debug. No handlers are removed, and a handler is only added if no handlers are already configured.

This should include general wsgi middleware, and logging.

Also, integrations for django and flask would be good.

Currently, when syncing a pair, `vdirsyncer` asks for confirmation prior creating a new collection in a storage.

It would be nice to have a command line argument like `--batch` or `--force`, which assures that `vdirsyncer` will never expect any input. If input is really really required, it should fail.

A use case would be a cron job, which mirrors a remote source to a local one (e.g. offline contacts).

As reported at https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/the-error-was-pluginerror-there-has-been-an-error-in-parsing-the-file/33792, Certbot currently errors out when parsing the following snippet of an Apache configuration file:
```
RewriteEngine On
RewriteMap symlinks prg:/var/www/redirector.pl
RewriteCond ${symlinks:%{HTTP_HOST}} !=""
RewriteRule ^/(.*) http://${symlinks:%{HTTP_HOST}}/$1 [R=301,L]
```
The line causing the problem is:
```
RewriteCond ${symlinks:%{HTTP_HOST}} !=""
```
Apache doesn't have a problem with this syntax.

cc @domcleal

issue_title: pip failing to run git in docker
issue_body:
When running tests with our `docker-compose` development setup, you see output like:
```
lint installed: Error [Errno 2] No such file or directory while executing command git config --get-regexp remote\..*\.url,cannot determine version of editable source in /opt/certbot/src (git command not found in path),Error [Errno 2] No such file or directory while executing command git rev-parse,Error [Errno 2] No such file or directory while executing command git rev-parse,Error [Errno 2] No such file or directory while executing command git rev-parse,Error [Errno 2] No such file or directory while executing command git rev-parse,Error [Errno 2] No such file or directory while executing command git rev-parse,acme==0.13.0.dev0...
```
This confuses new developers (and me until I figured out what was going on). This is `pip` noticing that the files installed in editable mode are part of a `git` directory and trying to use it to track changes. We have `.git` in our `.dockerignore`, but because we mount the cloned Certbot repo on the host inside the docker container, `.git` is available inside of `Docker`.

Perhaps we should install `git` in `Docker-dev`. Is the difference in `git` version between the host and container a possible problem if we do this? At the very least, we should document this behavior to avoid confusion.

issue_title: Start_tls should be used instead of ssl
issue_body: SSL is deprecated for LDAP version 3. start_tls should be used instead.

issue_title: Error: the JSON object must be str, not 'bytes'
issue_body:
I followed the dirrections here to try and program a TinyFPGA BX that I just got and when I get to the upload step it errors with `Error: the JSON object must be str, not 'bytes'`. I guess this is some random Python issue?

What happened:
Uploading the example project errors out with `Error: the JSON object must be str, not 'bytes'`

What should happen:
The project should upload and the FPGA should run it.

Platform:
`uname -a` : `Linux DESKTOP-DEBIAN 4.9.0-6-amd64 #1 SMP Debian 4.9.88-1+deb9u1 (2018-05-07) x86_64 GNU/Linux`

Steps to reproduce: (or what I did)
`sudo apt-get install python3-pip`
`sudo pip3 install --upgrade --no-cache-dir apio==0.4.0b3 "tinyprog>=1.0.9"`
`apio install system scons icestorm iverilog` (since drivers does not exist)
`apio drivers --serial-enable`
`tinyprog --update-bootloader`
Download atom-amd64.deb
`sudo dpkg -i atom-amd64.deb`
Install `apio-ide` in atom.
`git clone https://github.com/tinyfpga/TinyFPGA-BX.git`
`cp TinyFPGA-BX/apio_template blink_project`
Open `blink_project` with `atom`.
Upload.
Error happens here.




Issue originally reported to TinyFPGA, found to be an APIO issue so I am riposting it here.
tinyfpga/TinyFPGA-BX#2

issue_title: Look into using xcom for non-primitive types
issue_body: Currently xcom is used for primitive types (strings) and small bits of information, but we could potentially use this for communicating whole objects from one task instance to another. Risk is that these objects are most likely pickled under the covers (since they are communicated via the db), so we need to be careful.

```python

Registered template _ah/api/project/v1/endpoint under method post (/base/data/home/apps/s~app/v2.405240549392103317/lib/endpoints_management/control/service.py:240)

```

I see lots of above debug logs. We have 200+ endpoints and each one of them are showing in single api call logs - which suppresses my actual logs in that single api call logs. This was not the case with old v1. 

Please remove this unnecessary or non-interested logs.
![windows_error](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/3657779/20854190/b3066838-b92c-11e6-99de-52ec9c5f667b.png)

https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/e5817b598028afce52d7a1bcf07e2fef67ed035e/raiden/raiden_event_handler.py#L251

We need tests and documentation and should coordinate the exposed functions with #81 in order to close #10.

issue_title: Include Synapse as dev requirement once it supports Python3
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

At the moment Synapse (the Matrix Homeserver implementation written in Python) doesn't support Python 3 yet. From looking at the GitHub repo it seems they are actively working on getting support ready, however.

Due to that we currently have to [jump](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/890f57ea50daf8992c4844e99c5925d61b738de6/raiden/tests/conftest.py#L67-L72) through [some](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/e66612b9a15f017ced8f8dc00ea29c8646161d86/tools/install_synapse.sh) annoying [hoops](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/890f57ea50daf8992c4844e99c5925d61b738de6/raiden/ui/cli.py#L891-L902)  to get a local synapse server running for the tests as well as the smoketest. 

Once Synapse Python 3 support lands we should add synapse to `requirements-dev.txt` and simplify the test fixtures as well as the smoketest configuration.
